is important when using a microscope? How can you use this to help improve your results?

1st Dec 2017

Magnification is important when using a microscope as it allows you to see things that are much smaller than you could with the  human eye.

Resolution is important when using a microscope because it is the ability to see detail in two things that are very close to each other.

To help improve results when using a microscope you can make sure that you wear appropriate eye gear, you could also make sure you have a good understanding of how everything works and try to keep the area as dark as possible while you are working so you can better see under the microscope.Dismiss Notice

is the difference between the terms Christian Fundamentalism and Evangelicalism?

With the rise of the Religious Right in America, the terms Christian Fundamentalism and Evangelicalism have become more frequently used and often conflated in the media. Yet, there is a considerable difference between these two terms.

Evangelicalism refers to a branch of Protestant Christianity that emphasizes the role of the individual's personal, voluntary faith in accepting the redemptive power of Jesus Christ for salvation, a supreme authority of the Bible, the belief in the necessity of personal salvation as opposed to a salvation through one's good works, a distinction between faith and religion, the importance of God acting in one's life to bring about salvation, and stressing the need for a more active role of Christians in society and politics.

Christian Fundamentalism, on the other hand, is a movement in conservative Christianity that arose in the late 19th century—a reaction to the liberalism and modernism that were growing and becoming prevalent in the mainline denominations of the United States. The fundamentalist-modernist controversy led to the publication of the fundamentalist magazine The Fundamentals from 1909 to 1915. This movement was defined by a set of theological beliefs, particularly the

1. Keep a Track of Your Diet:

It is not just about how much you eat but what you eat that makes a significant difference. Consume more veggies, fruits, lean proteins, and whole grains. These are rich in nutrients and antioxidants that can nourish your body and prevent it from various ailments. Avoid junk and unhealthy foods as they are high in unhealthy fats, salt, and sugar, which can hurt your body and make you feel low.

2. Drink Adequate Water:

Water is the chief component of our body, and we require it in abundance to remain healthy. Stay hydrated throughout the day by drinking ample water.     While we are all busy using our intellects to dissect what Mr. Allen is saying and making our arguments and counterarguments, there is a huge point that nobody has addressed. And that is the idea of "a life of purpose".

    Who is to define the "purpose" of your life.
    While I agree that blind faith can be a crutch, what about faith in yourself, your abilities and your talents?

    If you have faith in yourself and have a sense of direction in this thing called Life, you will come up with your own sense of "purpose".
    Without that faith and sense of direction, you may well find your feet on a different path.

    Now I am not going to go into a religious diatribe here about what that faith might be. I will however make the point that if you leave it up to others to define the purpose of your life, you had best be sure you want it defined that way.

is a good time to start the search. The more time you spend gathering information and contacting lenders, the better.

Here are the initial steps you need to take in the student loan application process.

1. Understand the Different Types of Student Loans

There are two types of federal student loans: subsidized and unsubsidized. With a subsidized loan, the government pays the interest on your loan while you’re in school and during your six months post-graduation grace period. An unsubsidized loan allows you to start accumulating interest on your loan immediately. You have the option to pay the interest while you’re in school, or you can let it accumulate and be added to the total amount you owe after graduation.

2. Fill Out the FAFSA

The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) is the key to obtaining federal student loans. It’s also the determining factor for whether or not you are eligible for other forms of financial aid, like grants and work-study programs.

When you fill out the FAFSA, you’ll be asked for information about yourself, your family, and your financial situation. The form is used to calculate your Expected

is it possible to have only 1 active tab (or window) at a time?

I find it extremely distracting to have multiple web browser, xterm, or GUI window open and visible. It's not just the usual problem that these background windows can become a clutter if I move them around, but rather I find looking at multiple open windows very distracting. It's like when I'm talking to someone, I would like them to have my full attention and not have other people popping up, vying for my attention while I'm in the middle of doing something else.

Yes, I can switch between multiple windows with Alt-Tab all the time, but I'd rather not.

So are there window managers that have options to either make it so only the current active window gets rendered (and the rest are just frozen), or is there some other way to hide all windows except the current one and then bring back one at a time, when I need it?

Update: Thanks for your answers so far. I'm actually interested (though not requiring it) in a solution that's usable under Ubuntu.

is the problem with the P5 (and its replacements like the 5500, 5600P, 5700, 5800, 5850 and 5900)? The P5 is a chipset designed for frontside bus speeds equal to or faster than 800MHz. But Intel's i7 CPUs never reached those speeds, and the fastest i5 and i7 CPUs available in 2010 were stuck at 800MHz or slower. As a result, the P5 is in a weird position - all its features and capabilities are wasted on the slow CPUs it had to pair with. This makes the P5 a difficult chipset to recommend for new builds.

A tantrik baba or tantrika babi is usually a spiritual guide or teacher who follows a religious and sometimes mystical tradition known as tantra. Tantra is a very old and wide-spread practice in Eastern religions, primarily Hinduism and Buddhism. It involves the use of rituals, spells, chants and special techniques to reach spiritual enlightenment or attain higher states of consciousness.

Tantrik babas are widely recognized as having a strong connection with the spiritual realm and being able to communicate and interact with spirits. They are also known to have knowledge of spells and rituals that can help people with specific problems or situation.

Editor's Note: In a world where climate change dominates political and personal decision making, it is critical that we understand the science and the implications for our future. How sensitive is our climate system? What will be the implications of different stabilization levels of atmospheric greenhouse gases? One of the most influential figures associated with climate change, equilibrium climate sensitivity, is central to the debate. Climate models vary in simulated climate sensitivity, making it even more uncertain. Our guest scientist, NCAR senior scientist Kevin Trenberth, and his colleagues John Fasullo and Jeffrey Kiehl, recently addressed this uncertainty in the journal Climate Dynamics. They analyzed 124 climate models that had been run with two different scenarios, an increase in greenhouse gases alone (twice pre-industrial levels) and a full Earth system model run with increasing greenhouse gases, human aerosol emissions, and increasing solar variability. Here, Trenberth discusses the significance of their findings and the challenges associated with understanding and predicting climate change.

Climate sensitivity refers to how much the Earth will warm in response

set print asmdebug=1

Minecraft only allows for two colors on a button, Power and Off. The button does not have a down state like redstone comparators (on and off). If you want a button with two states, something that looks like the "Press to open - Press to close" chests, I'd suggest using two adjacent pistons and some redstone to create a sort of piston clock mechanism that moves the pistons out at power and then into the ground when not powered.

If you still want a button with two colors that you can click and change, I'd suggest making one side of the button out of one block, like redstone, then putting another block, like wool, over it, making it one block thick

The web developer industry is highly saturated due to a few reasons:

1. Anyone can become a web developer today. All you need is a computer and an internet connection, and you can find plenty of resources online (tutorials, code examples, paid web development courses, etc.) to get started with web development. These resources did not exist in the numbers they are available today a few years ago, so it's much easier for new people to enter web development.

2. Web development is considered a fun, creative, and interesting field. Many people are attracted to becoming web developers because it allows them to express their creativity through coding and building websites. This leads to many new people entering the industry, increasing the saturation.

3. Companies hire a lot of web developers, meaning there is demand for web developers. With demand comes the supply. Many people find out about this demand and they all try to jump in as web developers to meet that demand, leading to the web developer market being overly saturated.

My New York Psychologist Column: Intuitions Are Useful, But Don't Confuse Them With Reality

I have an article out today on the New York Psychologist website.  I suggest you check it out.

The column for this month discusses the relationship between intuition and judgment. Specifically, the title question: Does intuition make us better judges of other people?

A large majority of the participants in a survey said that they believe that people who are "tuned into their intuition" are wiser judges of other people. These people would think twice before going against their gut instincts when making judgments about others. This is interesting because most of the time people go against their gut when forming first impressions about others, and this probably isn't too helpful.

is a way of expressing the language of web content. The value of the hreflang attribute is an ISO 639-1 language code, e.g. "en" for English or "fr" for French. To specify a regional dialect, you can add a second code separated by a hyphen, e.g. "en-GB" for British English or "en-US" for American English.
,[@hreflang] is specified to indicate the language of every link of a list, instead of specifying it for each individual link.

Basic example

<link rel="alternate" hreflang="en" href="https://www.example.com/page/1" />
<link rel="alternate" hreflang="fr" href="https://www.example.com/page/2" />

Example using @hreflang

<link rel="alternate" hreflang="en" href="https://www.example.com/page/1" />
<link rel="alternate" hreflang="fr" hreflang="fr-fr" href="https://www.example.com/page/2" />

is there any difference between

Is there a significant difference between a new-school and old-school game shop? I'm interested but curious...

share|improve this question
up vote 13 down vote accepted

The primary difference, as you suggest, is age with a lot of spillover into the styles of product each tends to carry.

Old-School Game Shops

These tend to focus on "core" product from whatever was current during the 90s and early 2000s. You will find a lot of focus on older products, especially for older systems, but there is as much if not more of a focus on current offerings. They tend to be very, very system-specific - you're not going to see a lot of D&D 4e and D&D 3.5 side by side, for example, and don't expect to find a lot of anything else. They also tend to be very focused on miniatures and accessories - you will find a wide range of miniatures and gaming accessories, and dedicated tables for play.

New-School Game Shops

These tend to be more broad-based, including a wider variety of games, and including more system support for a wider variety of current

What is a Relay for Life event?
Relay for Life is a worldwide event that raises money for the 2001 Walker/Marathon Strong Against Cancer campaign. During Relay for Life, teams of 12 people pledge to stay awake for 24 hours (usually walking or jogging for most of the time and resting as a team decides) to support a cause and raise funds towards the fight against cancer.
How does a Relay work?
There are usually two different Relays held one in May for breast cancer awareness and education and another one in September for all types of cancer. It is easy to participate. Just pick which Relay you would like to participate in and sign up as an individual or as a team. You can then solicit donations online, by mail, or in person to raise money for the 2002Walker/Marathon Against Cancer campaign. During the event, you attend the designated time slot and stay awake for 24 continuous hours - usually walking or jogging for most of that time. Meaning, the rich and the poor share the same data points. The rich poor person, a Bentley, goes over the same speed bumps as a poor-person car; they use the same sidewalks, drive on the same roads.

In fact, I’ve said that if you want to measure inequality, you would need to get new data, such as the number of expensive cars vs cheap cars pulled over for speeding, or the number of sidewalk hot-dog vendors who sell to the rich or the poor, and so on.

But with the news this week of Prince William and Kate Middleton tying the knot in a much-awaited white (royal) wedding, we have a perfect showcase of how the same event can tell us as much about inequality as anything else.

The largest living reptile is the saltwater crocodile (Crocodylus porosus). They can grow up to 23 feet (7 meters) long and weigh over 2,000 pounds (900 kg). They are also the largest and most dangerous crocodile species in the world.

is the World Series of Poker? 
World Series of Poker (WSOP) is a series of poker tournaments held once a year in Las Vegas, Nevada. The tournaments are held in six different formats: No-Limit Hold’em, Pot-Limit Hold’em, Omaha High, Draw, Seven Card Stud and Razz. Each year, players from all over the world compete to win the coveted title of World Series of Poker Champion and take home millions of dollars in prize money. The WSOP is considered to be the premier poker tournament series in the world. The first WSOP was held in 1970, and has been held annually every summer since, except for one year from 1980 to 2004. Since 2005, the WSOP has been held in July.

is still in the laboratory stage. Here the team discusses why this technology is so exciting, and what hurdles they still need to overcome.

Why is using solar energy to extract CO2 so appealing?

Carbon capture and storage (CCS) has the potential to make power stations significantly more efficient and less polluting, but it is expensive. The great appeal of our technology is that it requires no electricity, so it is able to capture CO2 at a much lower cost than conventional systems, making it a much more viable option on a large scale.


What exactly does the system do?

The idea is to use sunlight to heat a selective solar absorber, which in turn heats a fluid. This fluid then heats another fluid that is in thermal contact with a filter filled with carbonate ions. The CO2 present in the gas produced by the power station diffuses through the filter and reacts with the carbonate ions to form bicarbonate ions, which then can be collected and stored.

What do you see as the main challenges in realizing this system?

There are a variety of materials of which you can make filters for this kind of application – including metal-organic compounds, metal hydrides and metal-carbon complexes

is the big deal about this? (Score 1) 307

by jfengel (#49181631) Attached to: Apple Bans "Real Apps" From Utilities That Use iOS Settings

The thing that's news is the change in policy, not the policy itself. They've always been fairly strict about only allowing utilities with a specific definition of "utility", and while they'd let some in the past that blurred the line with apps, they apparently feel the need to clarify now.

There's more news value in the fact that they're changing the rules than in the new rules themselves, which just redefine what a utility is. The rule doesn't make any sense: if something requires user input and does not provide a service, it's not a utility. It's a crappy, useless app that they ought to reject on general principles. They just need to reject them outright, rather than trying to draw distinctions about which apps they'll allow and which apps they won't.

The problem with software development is not so much in technical debt (which can be managed and even reduced), but in the fact that a software project is never really finished, it just reaches a release date before any foreseeable end of features. This leads to a constant pressure to introduce changes, but the development organization is anything but constant.

It´s not unusual for programmers to sit in the same office for 2-3 years in a row, before they have the chance to really focus on eliminating technical debt. Of course even a single programmer could try to set apart time for this, but chances are very low that this can be done consistently over a longer period.

Our existential questions expert and philosopher, Uta Franco, is here to answer your most profound queries. This week she addresses the nature of happiness and suggests ways to make our lives more meaningful.

Published on September 13, 2013
Uta Franco
Uta Franco

Is it possible to be happy all the time?

In short, no. Happy people often deny themselves the simple pleasure of sadness by trying to get over it as fast as possible, which ends up making them feel like an inconvenience in their own lives. It’s important to accept negative feelings and work through them in order to eventually reach the light. Don’t be too hard on yourself, but also don’t let yourself slip into a destructive way of life. It’s all a balance.

What is the meaning of life?

People often think that there must be some secret to a happy and successful life, but in reality there isn’t a secret – there is just living your own life and trying to find your balance. is actually a small-scale nuclear explosion.

In the 33 kilometer-radius, 10-16 kilometer-deep, cylindrical hole in the ground of Mount St Helens, there are many tons of (mostly) unused nuclear warheads, along with a few hundred tons of conventional explosives, most of which is now melted, mixed and spread all around the blast zone. There may also be hundreds to thousands of tons of nuclear waste involved, including unused fissile material, neutron sources, etc., but I won’t hazard a guess as to how much.

On the surface, most notably the north dome, there are thousands of tons of molten rock, soil and debris that was blasted to the surface. Most of the other ejecta is in the form of very fine particles which are mostly in the blast zone as dust and ash.

Overall, the blast from Mount St Helens looks to be, perhaps, only on the scale of 1% to 5% as energetic as your hypothetical 1 kiloton nuclear explosion. The difference is mostly due to the relatively small scale of the eruption and the fact that the actual explosion mostly melted and spread out rock, soil, etc, rather than throwing it directly away. is not exactly a good movie. Based on the true story of the eponymous cargo ship, abandoned mysteriously in the Atlantic Ocean in 1872, the legend has spawned numerous theories and a wealth of fascinating folklore. Bess doesn't offer many new insights into this mysterious tale, but he does a commendable job of conjuring the bone-chilling, otherworldly horror that must have gripped the ship's crew in their final moments. is the word "unfortunately". If a statement contains "unfortunately" it is usually followed by something that is the opposite of fortune. I think it's funny how one little word can so easily change the whole tone of a sentence.

For example, take this statement: "I am going to the pool today." Now, this is usually a pretty happy statement. Unless the speaker doesn't like swimming, then it's a statement of dread. But when you change that one word to "unfortunately" you turn the whole thing into a negative. "Unfortunately, I am going to the pool today." Now the same statement, said with the same words has a totally different tone to it, and is usually a statement of dread.

It just goes to show, how one word can change everything!

is there a way to calculate the exact amount of unallocated space left on the RAID5 set in the md superblock for a device? Is it kept up to date?

share|improve this question

2 Answers 2

up vote 3 down vote accepted

I don't think this information is stored in the RAID superblock (though it may be wrong, I'm not an expert and it's a gut feeling), but you can check the available free space at runtime using the following command:

cat /sys/block/md0/size

This will output the current size of the RAID5 volume in 512B blocks.

Keep in mind that in a RAID-5, free space is a bit more complex than on a simple filesystem (since RAID-5 needs to reserve some space for bit error correction). The above method would only give you the absolute free space available, not the size of 'chunks' that can be allocated to files.

is the answer to the question "What would you do with an extra $400?"Liquid Measure Conversion Table

Because recipes are often measured by volume, it's very important to know conversions of commonly used liquid measures. The standard US system uses the 'cup' (c) as its main unit of volume. But one cup is equal to 8 fluid ounces or about 236.6cc. And 1 imperial fluid ounce is roughly equivalent to 29.6cc.

If you need to convert more than a few ounces at a time, a conversion table is a handy thing to have around. You can print it out and stick it next to your recipe book or scale. Or you can save this information to your Favorites.

The conversion table is organized as follows:

From To
teaspoon (tsp) tablespoon (tbsp)
fluid ounce (fl oz) cup (c)
pint (pt) quart (qt)
gallon (gal)
milliliters (ml)

For example, if you have a recipe that calls for 2 tablespoons chocolate chips and you only have a teaspoon measure, you would use the conversion table to find how many teaspoons of chocolate chips you should use

is very important to pay attention to your baby's bowel movements

A baby's bowel movements are like an adults. The food you eat gets turned into energy for the growth and development of your baby and the waste products are eliminated by the bowels. It is important that you pay attention to your baby's bowel movements. In the first month after birth, the baby's digestive system is not fully developed yet and the stools can be yellow, be like cottage cheese or contain little blood clots.

If you observe a change in the consistency or color of the baby's stool, it may indicate that the baby has problems with its feeding. So what does a normal stool look like? The first stools are called meconium, and are dark green and paste-like. It can be sticky and you may need to wipe the baby to ensure they are clean after going to the bathroom. As the baby starts taking in more milk, you will notice that the stool turns yellow and becomes looser, more watery.

A normal stool is yellow or tan in color and is odorless. It can be seedy or smooth and should sink easily. As your baby grows, it may have bowel movements 2-3 times a day, while others

up vote 6 down vote accepted

The most likely reason is that your hamster is trying to make itself a more comfortable home. By carrying around pieces of its toys and eventually hiding them in its nest, your hamster is gathering things it finds comfortable or familiar and bringing them to its nest to make it even more comfortable.

It could also be territorial behavior – hamsters are known to do this with certain objects. This could mean that your hamster views the toy as its possession, and is bringing it to its nest to protect it and ensure it's available for when it wants it.

Remember, hamsters are creatures of habit so if your hamster feels more comfortable with these objects around

To be more specific, the search for habitable planets has been an ongoing one since the dawn of time with some of the earliest telescopes used in this search going as far back as the early 17th century.

Fast forward four centuries and scientists finally have some very exciting news to share, thanks to the launch of space probes and telescopes such as the Kepler space telescope in 2009.

The search has finally been a success with several planets discovered since then that are located in the habitable zone of their solar systems. This means that these planets are neither too close or too far away from the star they orbit to sustain life of one form or another.

Since the discovery was announced in late 2013, one planet in particular captured many imaginations, especially in the media; Kepler-186f, which was discovered in Kepler’s first data release in 2009. This planet is roughly 490 light years away from Earth and, although slightly smaller than Mars, is made of rock. Kepler-186f is also the outermost planet in its solar system, meaning it remains in the habitable zone where, it is thought, the temperatures are stable enough for liquid water to exist.

is the difference between an IRA and a 401(k)?

Difference between IRA and 401k

The primary difference between an IRA and a 401(k) is that the former is an individual retirement savings tool, while the latter is for employees of a company or government entity. IRAs are self-directed retirement savings programs that can be opened at most banks and investment firms, while 401(k)s are employer-offered retirement savings programs that are often sponsored by the employee’s employer.

Add descriptive text for color

Both IRAs and 401(k)s are designed to help people save for retirement by sheltering funds from taxes until they are withdrawn. The traditional versions of both accounts allow people to defer paying income taxes on the money they contribute until they withdraw it in retirement. When people withdraw the funds, they typically pay income taxes at their current rate. Roth versions of these accounts allow people to pay taxes on their contributions up-front and then pay no taxes on their withdrawals.

The annual contribution limits for these accounts are set by the IRS and can vary from year to year. For 2021, the limit for traditional and Roth IRAs is $6,000 per year ($7,000 for people aged

is the distinction between a 'cantonal' bank and a 'municipal' bank?
PUBLISHED: February 27, 2019
BY: BankingQuestions.com

In the United States, the term 'cantonal' bank is not used because most U.S. banks operate under a federal system of government, whereas the term is used in the countries forming the European Union.

In a federal system, the powers of government are divided between the central government and the individual states. So, in the United States, the powers of the federal government are divided between the State and the federal government.

Here, banks are regulated and supervised by one of the Federal Reserve Banks, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, or the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, or by the individual states. The states have broad powers to regulate their banks, including the ability to set up charter offices to charter banks.

Under this system, there are no cantonal or municipal governments with powers to charter banks.

is the difference between a general partnership and a limited partnership?
A general partnership is a partnership in which all partners have personal liability for the debts and obligations of the partnership

A limited partnership is a partnership in which there are two classes of partners, general partners and limited partners. The general partners have personal liability for the debts in obligations of the partnership. The limited partners, on the other hand do not have personal liability for the debts and obligations of the partnership

*A general partner is an partner in a general partnership or a limited partner in a limited partnership that has personally guaranted the partnership debts
What are the advantages and disadvantages of a partnership?
Advantages of a Partnership
1. Flexibility- a partnership can be formed quickly and without a contract

2. Pass Through Taxation- a partnership is not taxed as a separate entity profits and losses are passed through to the partners

3. Personal Liability- partners have unlimited liability for business losses

4. Personal Control- profits and losses pass through to the partners and decisions are made by all or most of the partners

Disadvantages of a Partnership
1. Unlimited Personal Liability- partners have unlimited liability for the debts and obligations of the partnership

I feel privileged to have a front-row seat to a revolution of the 1999 version of Java, a revolution that, for the most part, is happening under the radar. A revolution happening where it matters the most, right in the mainstream platforms, driven by open-source projects and embraced by mainstream Java developers.

Let’s go back to 1999

Going back to 1999 is both going back in time and a way of acknowledging a tradition and its fruits. That year, a JVM language named JML was brought to the world by a team of Carnegie Mellon University researchers, a team that included Benjamin C. Pierce, one of the most influential figures in modern type theory. JML was a formal specification language for Java, and it was used both for proving the correctness of Java programs and for providing specifications for libraries written in Java.

JML was powerful and interesting, but it was also not meant to replace Java or to be a niche language for academic circles. JML was born to improve the quality of Java applications, and to do that, it needed a way to integrate seamlessly with Java code.

share|improve this question

3 Answers 3

up vote 38 down vote accepted

is the meaning of the word 'yol' in this line:

— by Meri Yar (Yanar)

The meaning is 'Yollari yok (without path there), yok (is not)'

Yollari yok meaning is 'There is no path', yok in general sense means 'not there' or 'nothing' or 'none')

As for the use of two 'yok', it's because Yar (derived from Yanar) is a name meaning 'light' (and by implication 'path') and hence is a play on words.

(Meri Yar is an Iranian poetess from the 13th century AD)

In this case 'yollari yok' means 'without path there' and 'yok' (nothing, not there) is used for poetic purposes to give twofold meaning: 1 with you (path and light) there, 2 without you (light, path) there is nothing.

is the purpose of the "check_temp.sh" script in etc/init.d on raspberry pi?

I know that in the past some devices had problems if the cpu temperature got to high.
Are there such problems with the raspberry or a service running with a specific temperature is aborted due to a high cpu temperature?

The script calls

/usr/bin/check_temp 40 80

which are most likely temperatures in Celsius.

The first is the minimum CPU core temperature, below which the processor is shut down.
The second is the maximum CPU core temperature, above which the processor is shut down.

This is done to prevent the thermal event (shutting down of the cores) which causes the SD-card to be ejected and the running of the raspberry stopped.

The temp monitoring can be done via

service check_temp status

is an article on some of the basics of the English language: articles. In the English language, articles are words that give more information about a noun. There are two articles in the English language: the definite article and the indefinite article.

Definite article and Indefinite article
The definite article is "the". It is used to define a specific noun that is being referred to. It is used before singular and plural nouns. For example; "I saw the car"; "I saw three the houses" The Indefinite article is "a" and "an". They are used to define a word that is not specific, and we are not sure if someone knows which one we are talking about. It is used before singular uncountable nouns, like: "I want a book", "please give me a apple", "I saw a car".

  • Ensure your app is up-to-date. Inactivity, or failing to respond to customer feedback with new features or improvements, can cause customer dissatisfaction.

  • Invest in your app’s design. The presentation and design of the app can give a customer a positive or negative impression. If the design is poor, that could lead to negative word of mouth and potentially a loss of customers to the small number of competitors in your market.

Is there a limit to how many times you can use any given keyword on a page and still have it be recognized by search engines? What if the search engines see the same keyword repeated too many times on one page? Would it become penalized?
United Kingdom
PixelPals Website
posts: 11681 United Kingdom
Joined: 8. Aug 07, United Kingdom
8 years ago
Using the same keyword too many times in your on-page content will no longer help your rankings. In fact it's thought to be a negative factor. Search engines no longer look at the content of a page in the same way they used to. Instead of counting how many times a keyword is used, they use semantic searching to interpret the meaning of the words. So instead of focusing on keywords, they focus on the whole concept. It's best to focus on writing natural, readable content. Make sure you have relevant heading tags and alt attributes though.

is it the end of the world?”: how the BBC’s language use in relation to climate change has evolved over five decades

From The Conversation UK, 8 April 2019

By Anna Maltby, University of East Anglia

On December 2, 1968, the BBC broadcast a special report on the threat of climate change. The report, entitled Is it the end of the world?, discussed the impact of increased carbon dioxide in the atmosphere due to human activity. The programme was a response to the first international scientific assessment of the climate question, published by the US government in 1967.

On April 5, 2019, the BBC Four network broadcast a dramatised documentary, Climate of Hate, about the life of climate activist Greta Thunberg. Greta Thunberg has become both an international celebrity and an icon for global youth environmental activism. Thunberg first gained attention for her regular school strikes outside the Swedish parliament to demand immediate climate action.

There is some symmetry between these two cases. Both shows discuss a climate emergency. But the language used by the BBC has changed substantially in its reporting on climate change over the last 50 years.

Generally, it has more to do with the destination country and its relationship with alcohol than where you buy the alcohol or whether it's in an unopened bottle.

There are three basic categories of alcohol on airplanes and in airports:

  1. Alcohol that is completely prohibited. This is most common in very conservative Muslim and religious countries. There are many examples of airlines that completely prohibit alcohol on their flights to these destinations.
  2. Alcohol that you can buy, drink on the plane, and finish off (if you're brave enough), but cannot take with you in your checked luggage or carry on. The idea is that it's considered "for use during the flight."
  3. Alcohol that you can buy and carry with you, either in checked luggage

is a free online platform for publishing, archiving, and sharing research data.

So what is "Research Data"?

Research data typically comes in two versions: "Research Data Objects" (RDOs) and "Raw research data."

An RDO is a curated set of data that tells a story about a research problem and its solution. RDOs are often stored in a specialized database, or sometimes presented in a spreadsheet, and are typically accompanied by a brief explanation or narrative that documents how the data were collected, analyzed, and interpreted. RDOs are typically generated by researchers for their use and that of other researchers, with all relevant data included (not discarded).

Raw research data consists of the original measurement data in its full glory, perhaps in its original form after being generated by a device (such as a geiger counter or seismograph) or after being created by a researcher (such as after being recorded in a data collection notebook). Rarely are all of the raw measurement data from a particular research project needed by another researcher, but sometimes this data is valuable.

Why keep Research Data?

Notes on The Efficient Amount of Time to Look for A Lost Key
The answer is that there is some amount of time after which you cannot have possibly misplaced the key somewhere in your apartment. Thus, beyond that point you can rule out the possibility of it being in your apartment and can get on with the business of buying a new lock.

Alternatively, you can say that the answer is an expected cost/benefit calculation. Beyond a certain amount of searching there is an increasing chance you are wasting your time. In the limit there is a 100% chance that you are wasting your time and a decreasing increment of marginal benefit to additional searching. Thus, at some point you should stop.

Nanoparticles are defined as particles smaller than 100 nanometers (100 billionths of a meter), but there are infinite types of nanoparticles based on their source, chemical composition, and application. Some of the most commonly used nanoparticles include Quantum Dots, Carbon Nanotubes, Graphene, and Metallic nanoparticles, each with its unique properties making it suitable for certain applications.

is used when you want to describe the location of a place, in an area around you. The term "вокруг" seems to also fit for it since it has the meaning around, but I guess they can't be used interchangeably. So when to use "около" and when "вокруг"?

share|improve this question
up vote 11 down vote accepted

Both expressions can be used when talking about the location, but they are not interchangeable.

"Вокруг" refers to an area on a more or less defined perimeter. It typically contains a central point or entity around which all other locations are distributed.

"Около", on the other hand, is more general. It describes a surrounding area without a necessary central point. It can be used to point some vicinity or area which is not a whole, but rather a part of something larger.

So, if you want to describe your house located somewhere in a town, you should specify more what do you mean -- are there other houses (approximately) around yours? If yes, you should use "вокруг". If not, use "около".

But both can be used in other contexts:

The proc filesystem doesn't really "provide" system information, such
as syslog messages or /var/log/messages, or what you'd expect
to see on a web or NFS export.

The proc filesystem is not really for "the system" at all, it is
mainly for the kernel, to give it an interface with the user-space
world in the way a command-line interface would provide but more
flexible. It mainly gives access to kernel

No, it is not possible to install the virtual machine additions for Mountain Lion on a physical machine. The additions are designed to be installed only on virtual machines, specifically those running Windows operating systems.

If you need to install additional software or drivers that the default Software Properties provide, you can still use the installation file for 32-bit Windows on a 64-bit operating system. You will need to extract the files from the VAA using a program like WinRAR and manually copy them to the VirtualBox additions folder on your virtual machine.

To get a sense of how raw the underlying hardware is, it is possible to run the operating system without an operating system. This type of system is called a bare machine.

On x86-64, a bare machine loads its instructions and initial data from a “boot ROM” on the motherboard. is the new 'It' drug and why young people are dying from it

After a spate of dangerous and often deadly encounters with drugs in recent months, one substance has become the latest — and possibly the most dangerous — craze amongst young people.

What is the new 'It' drug and why young people are dying from it

Krokodil, which is actually a street name for desomorphine, began to appear two or three years ago in Russia. It has only recently started to rear its ugly head in western countries. The drug was developed in the 1920s as a morphine substitute but was soon withdrawn from the market. It is described as seven to eight times more potent than morphine, a ten times greater risk of addiction and 35 times more toxic to the body thanheroin.

Krokodil, which is cheaper than heroin, is homemade, using either codeine or over-the-counter cold medicines, which are then mixed together with a cocktail of toxic materials such as iodine, hydrochloric acid, hydrochlorozince and paint thinner. The concoction is injected directly into the bloodstream, resulting in the injection site turning scaly and green before falling off, often leaving the bone exposed.

posted on Jul, 2 2014 @ 07:34 PM
a reply to: Wrabbit

While I understand the search for answers and how our bodies work, and how to maintain them in the best way possible, I personally don't think that it's okay to experiment on yourself by not eating.

I really admire people who can do it, but I just could never put myself through that.

When you go into survival mode like that, your hunger for food turns into a hunger for fuel. The part of your brain that craves nutrients and the "pleasure" of your next meal isn't fully engaged as long as your body is "okay" with not eating. It's more about your body telling its self that it needs to conserve energy and keep fat in order to survive.

Or it could be that you ride on the pig and hence it means to ride on another's bicycle. –  Noah Feb 11 '13 at 13:23

2 Answers 2

up vote 6 down vote accepted

No direct evidence linking the modern figurative senses of piggyback to pigs or packs exists, so currently the derivation is uncertain. There are three possible meanings of piggyback:

1. Originally, piggy-back was a method of carrying a pig on your back to prevent it from escaping. The term piggy-back dates back to 1567 when it appeared as piggain-backe. Pergaines, a pig in Dutch, plus back, hence piggyback.

is a question I get all the time so I thought I would devote some space to the topic.

Q: What is a good way to work abduction exercises in to my current workouts?

A: First off let me define exactly what abduction is. Abduction simply means movement away from the midline or center of the body. You can think of it as lateral or side to side movement such as with a lateral lunge or a clam shell.

Now for ways to integrate abduction training into your current workout routine. Most people are more than familiar with quad and hamstring dominant workouts such as: Squats, Lunges, Deadlift, Hamstring curls, etc. However, not as much emphasis is usually placed on the hip abductor muscles.

For a well rounded and effective program I recommend at least twice as many exercises for the hip abductors. I can usually count on one hand the number of exercises I see in most peoples routines that target this important area.

To incorporate this I usually suggest separating out hip dominant exercises for another day and then making sure to include a good deal of single leg work for the hip abductors during days that are not totally focused on the split.

    I find studying a new subject very time consuming as well as stressful because there is so much material. However, I don't think either quantity or quality should be overlooked. is the right time to start investing in shares or stocks?

Updated on August 21, 2016

There is no magic number, date or qualification that determines the good time to start investing in shares or stocks. Anyone, at any age and from any walk of life, can, and should start investing.

There’s no reason to wait

Do not wait until you have saved enough money, have an emergency fund in place or until any perceived financial challenges have been fixed first. While some of these things are valuable and it may be prudent to have a sound financial plan in place before moving forward with investing, none of these factors should prevent you from investing.

The power of time

Your ability to invest at any time and age can be amplified by the concept of time on your side. It is called compounding, and this effect can work for or against you. For instance, if a young adult begins investing $100 per month from the age of 20 for just 5 years before deciding to quit, they will end up with significantly less money at retirement compared to someone who starts investing the same amount at the same rate but manages to keep investing for 40 years.

Tips To Help You Choose The Best Air Purifier For Your Needs

Air purifiers can be great for keeping your air clean, and they make a big difference. So how do you choose the best air purifier? Here are some tips to help you make that decision.

Check the technology used

Air purifiers today use a wide variety of technology to clean the air. Some work by trapping particles, while others work by killing biological pollutants. Air ionizers are one type that create negatively charged ions that trap pollutants. HEPA air purifiers are another type that is very effective at removing particles from the air. If you want an air purifier that can handle biological pollutants, you’ll need to find one that uses UV light or other methods of killing germs.

Make sure it’s large enough

You need to make sure that the air purifier you choose has the capacity to handle the square footage of the room where you are planning to use it. Many air purifiers list their coverage area specifications. If the air purifier isn’t large enough, it won’t be effective at removing pollution from your air.

is an example of a common logical fallacy in the search for health information.

“Do you want to reduce your risk of heart disease, arthritis and cancer? If so, this new product is for you. Its unique blend of oils, enzymes, minerals and vitamins is scientifically formulated to help your body achieve optimum health.”

Although this seems like a persuasive message, there are a few logical fallacies. First, a list of conditions is named, but no specific benefits are mentioned for each condition. Second, there is no mention of the specific components that provide these benefits, much less the quantities needed to be effective. Third, the reference to “science” is used as a general appeal to authority without specifying what kind of scientific evidence supports the product’s claims.

The information provided suggests that the product is effective for many conditions, but does not go into specifics. These types of claims are often found on advertisements for products, but they rarely provide enough detail to determine how effective these products may be.

Here are a few more logical fallacies that you may come across when evaluating health information:

Post Hoc, Ergo Propter Hoc

is not meant to replace your current security system. Rather, it is designed to enhance and improve it. Our security cameras can act as a constant security guard by monitoring your property and activities throughout the day and night.

Security cameras act as a strong visual deterrent against potential intruders due to their strong presence. However, this isn't the real value of them.

Surveillance Cameras as Evidence

One of the most important aspects of a security camera system is the ability to collect evidence in the event of an incident. If there is an attempted intrusion, or an actual intruder, an alarm alone can alert you, but it won't help the police capture the intruder. It would also be your word against the intruders.

A security camera, however, can capture video of the incident, and potentially the license plate of a getaway vehicle. This video can be turned over to the police to help them find and arrest any criminals involved.

Recordings of incidents around the property can also serve as evidence for insurance claims in case of damage to property.

As for the question of how important security cameras are for your business, it depends on what your business is and what you are looking to protect.

USENIX is entering a new era in technical conference management with the introduction of USENIX '06 Virtual Conference, which will take place June 27-July 1, 2006, at USENIX '06.

is used to diagnose the clinical presentation and cause of a stroke. An electrocardiogram (ECG) and blood tests are performed to identify underlying cardiac disorders or drug use as the cause of the stroke. Imaging studies including computed tomography (CT scan) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) provide detailed images of the brain, help differentiate between ischemic and hemorrhagic strokes, and identify areas of ischemia or infarction.
 Digital subtraction angiography (DSA) is used to visualize the blood vessels in the brain. DSA is usually performed in the emergency setting and can show the location of occlusion or rupture in patients with an ischemic or hemorrhagic stroke, respectively.

The most sensitive indicator of the time course of a stroke is the measurement of neurological deficit. Assessment Tools for Health System Evaluation (ATHES) scores and the Functional Independence Measure (FIM) are often used to evaluate cerebral palsy rehabilitation or stroke patients. These scores assess a patient's ability to complete certain tasks or movements and help track improvement over time.

The extent of diagnostic procedures used depends on the time elapsed since symptom onset and the patient's neurological condition.

What is iAppraisal?

iAppraisal is a revolutionary new app, which aims to connect employers and job seekers and help them find jobs and right talent respectively. But what makes iAppraisal special is its unique feature that offers employer’s data-driven talent hiring solution, which helps employers to set their pay scale in accordance with the current market trends and norms. This is the first of its kind, which not only make it unique and useful, but also beneficial for the people.

How does it work?

The working principle is very simple, all the employer has to do is to enter the details of the job in the app. The app not only takes into account the job role, but the educational qualification, skill sets, and experience required for it.

is a term used in the search engine optimization world. It describes a page on a website which ranks very high in Google search results and receives a high volume of organic search traffic.
Pages with high domain authority and a lot of backlinks are typically trusted more by users and are more likely to be shared.
Also known as link equity, refers to the amount of value or "power" one website passes onto another site via a link.
Backward Link
A link from a page on another site that points back to your own site. Also known as an "inbound link" or "incoming link." The more authoritative sites that link back to your website, the more reputable your site is in the eyes of search engines.
Backlinks refer to the quantity and quality of external websites that are linking back to your website. A backlink is a hyperlink on an external website that points to your website. Search engines consider backlinks to be an indicator of a website's popularity, which impacts its ranking in results pages. If your website is being referred to by multiple reputable sources, search engines are more likely to trust that your content is valuable and worthy of a high search engine ranking.

is important to know what they do and how they can impact your health.

What is cortisol?

Cortisol is a hormone made by the pancreas; it’s often known as the “stress hormone”. However, cortisol is essential for life. It regulates metabolism, helps your body cope with stress, maintains your immune function, and helps the body use protein, carbohydrates, and fats.

What happens when you have high or low cortisol?

When you have high cortisol, your body can experience several health complications. High cortisol over an extended period can result in weight gain, especially around the stomach. It can weaken your immune system, leading to more frequent illnesses. High cortisol can also affect your mood and cause anxiety. If your body has low cortisol for a prolonged period, you can experience low blood pressure, dizziness, fatigue, low blood sugar, and weight loss. Low cortisol can also result in mood changes, causing irritability and depression.

When to see your doctor

While it is normal to have fluctuations in your cortisol levels throughout the day, chronic fluctuations can be a cause for concern. You should schedule an appointment with your doctor if you are experiencing prolonged stress, anxiety, depression, or frequent illnesses. Additionally, if

Emotional Freedom Techniques, also known as EFT, is a form of psychological acupressure based on the ideas of Chinese medicine, psycho-pathology and the mind-body connection. EFT uses the fingertips to tap on specific acupuncture points, while focusing on and speaking to whatever negative emotions you may be experiencing. By stimulating specific acupressure points, EFT works to help restore the energy pathways, improve your emotional well-being and enhance your overall health.

is it that certain songs, movies, or pieces of art move us so deeply and evoke intense emotional responses that they are etched into our memories forever?

The answer to this question lies in the science of our brains and how they react to the art being created. It is a highly complex and intricate process that involves both emotion and cognition. In short, our brains are wired to feel and respond to art in ways that help us to understand and process complex emotions and experiences.

One of the best ways to understand this process is by examining the role of the prefrontal cortex in emotional experiences. Simply put, the prefrontal cortex is the part of our brain that integrates information from various parts of the brain, allowing us to experience different emotions and react accordingly. This area of the brain is responsible for processing complex emotions such as love, empathy, and even guilt, and allows us to take these emotions and use them to make decisions and guide our actions.

But the involvement of the prefrontal cortex in emotional experiences doesn’t end there. Another area of the brain that plays a crucial role in this process is the amygdala. This is the part of the brain responsible for processing basic emotions such as fear and happiness, and is closely connected to the prefrontal cortex. It

is it possible to display all entries from a table, but in a different format? For example, I have a page that is similar to an index page, but would also display the pages in a different format (maybe a list or something else).

Is there a way to accomplish this using Craft?

share|improve this question

To display all entries from a table, you should simply use:

{% set entries = craft.entries.section('mySection') %}

To specify the format you want to use, you can use Twig loops like this:

{% for entry in entries %}
    {{ entry.title }}
{% endfor %}

You can easily use these entries in a list, table, etc. You can find some inspiration from the Twig documentation

is a "Mini-Botox" and does it work on wrinkles? - San Diego, CA

Doctor Answers 12

Mini Botox injections are usually done for aesthetic purposes

"Mini-Botox" is not a medical term. It is a lay term used by some providers to describe a smaller amount of Botox used for aesthetic purposes, usually on the NYC east coast where there are different standards of care than in the west coast. For example, if you want to soften crow's feet but not paralyze your ability to show joy or express yourself around your eyes, you might get what they call a "mini" treatment. The average is around 20 units used for the face. It depends on the size of the person, location of injections and how visible and deep the lines are. A few units can be used for prevention and to smooth out the skin or a little more to see a change in muscle action. Botox results last 3-4 months so treatment is an ongoing process.

Is a Voting Station?
NewsLetter23rd March 2022

Is a Voting Station?

In today’s world, where technology has made such significant strides, voting has been modernized and has become more convenient and accessible through the online voting system. With this system, people no longer need to go to their local courthouse, stand in line and fill out ballots. Instead, they can now use their computer, tablet, or smartphone to vote from the comfort of their own home.

A voting station, which is also known as a polling booth, is a specific location where registered voters go to cast their vote. Prior to voting, these individuals must display their IDs at the polling station for verification, which helps keep the voting process honest and secure. Voting stations have always been associated with physical polling stations, but with the advent of online voting, they have started to adopt a new meaning.

When voting is conducted online, a voting station can now be an application or program where voters can open a web browser and go to the website to vote. Because this is an online system, a voter's information is securely saved until it's time to vote. By using a voting station, one’s vote will remain confidential,

Two loudspeakers arranged in a symmetrical arrangement and a control panel including (at least) a keyboard, a source selector and control buttons.


The sounds produced by the synthesiser are generated by an electronic oscillator, producing a continuous spectrum of frequencies and the selection of sounds (timbre) is made via the source selector of the control panel. The synthesiser is often (but not necessarily) connected to an amplifier and loudspeakers, as is the case with other musical instruments. is a very simple web framework that is built to be embedded.

Version Picture

The picture to the the left represents the dependencies between different parts of webjive. The numbers inside the boxes represent the modules number of lines of code.

1. http

The http module is the core of webjive. It provides an easy way to process web requests without having to create a lot of code. It handles the request parsing and dispatching process and supports all the features required by the HTTP/1.1 specification. It also supports both GET and POST methods.

2. request

The request module is built on top of http and adds support for more request parameters processing. It will parse GET and POST parameters regardless of the amount, whether they are signed or unsigned, and also has built in decoding for several of the most common encoding schemes used for request parameters.

3. response

The response module is used in conjunction with http and request. It abstracts the response writer. Which makes it very easy if you want to add support for a new response type like XML or SOAP. All you would need to do is implement a new writer object that takes care of writing the data to the output.

is the future tense in French?


Quick Answer

The future tense in French is formed with the help of two types of verbs: the auxiliary or helping verbs and the main or action verbs. The main verb in its basic form and the auxiliary verb "avoir" or "être" are used to create the future tense.

Continue Reading
What is the future tense in French?
Credit: Chris Clor Creative RF Getty Images

Full Answer

To form the future tense, follow these steps:

1. Identify the main verb and its tense.
2. Choose which auxiliary verb to use. "Avoir" is typically used for all irregular verbs and regular -er and -re verbs while "être" is used for regular -re verbs that don't conjugate with "avoir."
3. Conjugate the auxiliary verb in the future tense.
4. Conjugate the main verb in its future tense form.

Examples include:
- Je parlerai (I will speak)
- Tu écriras (You will write)
- Il, elle, on actionnera (He, she or it will act)

is possible to set up some commands which are always available, no matter which input mode I'm in.

I use the 1912-1938.el input method.

For example let's suppose I want the ¢ character to be always accessible, no matter the mode I am in.

Where should I put the command? And how should it look like?

share|improve this question

2 Answers 2

Putting your favorite characters as precomposed characters in your init file is definitely a better idea. This allows Emacs to know when it needs to use them and when not, and they won’t muck up cases such as searching, completion etc. They’ll also only take space as much as any other character.

(set-input-method "1938-1998")

(put '1938-1998 'char-charset-extension '(33 35 99 100 101 102 104 105 107 108 134 135 136 137 138))

(modify-syntax-entry ?¢ "w")

Your other option is to bind the compose sequence to the character.

(define-key input-decode-map [°] (kbd "C-q¢"))

is also one of the most important because it is directly related to the future success or failure of the project. Good communication is based on four essential elements: transparency, feedback, agreement on scope, and ongoing collaboration.

Transparency is a key element of good communication. It is the practice of making all the information relevant to the project available to all stakeholders. Without transparency, the team may not have access to the information they need to make informed decisions, and this can lead to misunderstandings, misinterpretations, and wasted time and resources. Transparency is also important for building trust and creating a collaborative environment. Team members are more likely to share their ideas and opinions, and to ask questions, if they know that all relevant information is being shared. This leads to a better understanding of the project and the work being done, and to improved problem solving and decision making.

Feedback is the process of responding to performance and reporting on how well the project is moving ahead. Providing both positive and negative feedback is important for the project team to understand how well it is performing and how it can improve. The feedback should be given regularly and should be specific and constructive, focusing on what was done well and what can be improved, rather than on the individual

is about the search for meaning by exploring the power of love.

As a young man, Richard was in an open relationship (one where the rule was you could have other lovers, but you needed the permission of the other person). He was involved in an intense love relationship with a woman who eventually went to another country as a missionary. Richard was very uncomfortable with the idea that the relationship might be over, but in a moment of clarity he broke up with her, not knowing at the time that she was on her way back from a distant mission, and on her way to him.

She returned, and soon enough was back in his arms, and the relationship resumed. Then, two years later, Richard had grown so uncomfortable with the open relationship, feeling like it wasn’t quite right for him, that he broke up with her (and had another girlfriend for a short time).

Then, as fate would have it, a year later she returned to him, asking for an opportunity to pursue a more traditional relationship. Richard was hesitant, but gave it a try. It worked out, for the most part, for a while. After a while he began to lose focus and became obsessed with other women, so she broke up with him

is the difference between the terms ‘femme’, ‘femme presenting’, ‘femme presenting genderqueer’ and ‘genderfluid femme’? I am having a lot of trouble keeping track of all of them.

Sure! Let’s break these down one by one.

1) Femme.

Femme is a term that is often used within queer communities to refer to someone who identifies as a woman or someone who’s assigned male at birth (AMAB) but who displays femininity in their expression and/or gender identity. The term comes from queer communities of colour and the radical lesbian movements of the past, who sought to reclaim femininity as a queer and subversive identity. It can be used as a noun or adjective, and it is not meant to imply a cisgender or heteronormative identity. So, a cisgender woman could be described as a femme woman, but it’s also perfectly valid for trans women, genderqueer and genderfluid people, and even AMAB individuals to identify as femme.

2) Femme presenting.

This is a slightly more restrictive term, that is sometimes used to refer to someone who is visibly

# Eliminating unwanted objects while keeping the shortest path
ERTYPE POINT1 = {x=0, y=0}
TYPE POINT2 = {x=0, y=1}

TYPE LINE = object
_x: int
_y: int
_len: int

def __init__(self, _x, _y):
self._x = _x
self._y = _y
self._len = min(_x, _y)
self.set_len()

def set_len(self):
_sum = self._x + self._y
self._len = min(_sum // 2, self._x, self._y)

def __repr__(self):
x = self._x
y = self._y
len_ = self._len
return f"LINE:{x}__{y}__len:{len_}"

is the future of the car? I believe it will be a network of “smart” vehicles, communicating on how to reach your destination the most efficient way. As more cars become internet connected, car companies, service providers and even local authorities will be able to optimize the cars routes.

In the future self-driving cars will not be a luxury but standard and it is expected that they will generate about 30% cuts in traffic congestion and 30%-50% drops in car costs. This means there will be fewer accidents, better air quality, less noise and more time for passengers.

The new internet of vehicles will not only allow cars to optimize routes and save time, but they will also connect to other connected objects, like home and work devices, enabling them to communicate with each other.  The connection of devices will enable smart management of home or work environment.

There are already now more than 200 car manufacturers developing autonomous cars and every major automaker now is making self-driving cars. At this point, it is no longer if, but when, this technology will be commonly adopted, and it will be the main force behind the biggest shake-up of the automotive industry we have ever seen.

text:
"Unemployment, Relative Price Dispersion and the Limits to Convergence: A US State Analysis"
Journal of Macroeconomics 47 (2016) 27-38

Abstract:
The literature has shown that convergence in per capita GDP between countries with initially high and low income differences is slow and imperfect. In this study, we test whether similar results hold for US states. In addition, we investigate if relative price dispersion, a measure of competition in an economy, has a quantitatively significant effect on the speed of convergence. To this end, we estimate a panel cointegration model with long-term equilibrium and short-term deviations for annual US states GDP data from 1960 to 2013. We find that convergence is slow and imperfect and, contrary to the international literature, the effect of initial standard deviation on convergence is quantitatively small and insignificant when relative price dispersion is controlled for. In other words, the degree of competition in the economy has a much larger effect on convergence than the initial inequality. The least competitive states (measured by the Herfindahl index) converge fastest, followed by medium competitive states and lastly, the most competitive states. This result is robust to the inclusion of additional variables such as the share of government expenditure in the economy, the real exchange rate and
prompt_prefix: "Unemployment, Relative Price Dispersion and

text:
Are film trailers spoiling movies?
In April 2012, the request with the 2nd highest number of hits in The Pirate Bay was not a porn or an anime, it was a TV-show, and not even a single episode, but the entire series finale. That show was called LOST.

And yes the same thing is happening as we speak this very moment. As far as movies are concerned, some would say that a film’s success or failure is determined by how well its trailers are received. Trailers tend to entice audiences to part ways with their precious disposable income as they leave the theater, or perhaps they do not. I shall leave it up to you to fill in the blanks. Yet, whether a movie fails or succeeds, it can’t be said that trailers are made only, or indeed mostly, as a form of promotion. They’re also a spoiler alert billboard, one that tends to ruin movies to the point there really’s no need to watch it.

So, should trailers be shorter? Or should potential movie goers be responsible for their own actions before watching them? Either or, or is there another way?

In case you're unfamiliar, PUBG was a sensation on the Xbox One and PC when it first came out, thanks to its focus on large-scale, open-world battles with friends. For those of you who want to add PUBG to your PC gaming library but haven't yet, you can find it on Steam for $30. This is also the minimum system requirement for the game. You'll need a pretty beefy rig in order to get the most out of PUBG, but we'll have a full guide on how to enjoy the game on mid-range hardware shortly.

text:
After the martyrdom of St. Boniface in 869, the search for a bishop to head the church in Germany and the surrounding countries intensified, particularly after the devastating raids of the 870's. A church council held in Frankfurt in 881 urged the selection of a successor for Boniface, who was already revered as a saint. The electors considered several candidates but finally turned to the archbishop of Mainz and insisted that he resign his other bishoprics in favor of the spiritual leadership of the Germans. Reluctantly, he agreed, and became the first archbishop of the newly instituted diocese of Magdeburg, exercising full episcopal authority over all Germany (Deutsche Kirche, 21-22).
---|--- A New Foundation for German Christianity ------------------------
The establishment of a distinct and unified German church had profound and far-reaching results in the spiritual, cultural, and political life of the German people. It placed the German church at the center of the Carolingian Empire and made it possible for the Germans living within this vast empire to maintain and develop their own traditions and way of life. At the same time, the unity of the German church facilitated close cooperation with the papacy and played an important role in the evolution of
prompt_prefix: After the martyrdom of St. Bonif

WordPress Plugins

We know what WordPress themes are, but this time we are discussing WordPress plugins. What are they and why are they important?

WordPress plugins add new features to the WordPress platform, allowing you to tweak and customize your site in any number of advanced ways. If you need a new feature and you can’t figure out how to code it, there’s probably a WordPress plugin that does it already.

Plugins can be used for any number of things (translation/multilanguage, ad revenue or traffic statistics, for example), but they also have a few downfalls. Plugins may make your site slower, increase security risks, or interfere with each other. To minimize these risks and to ensure that the plugins you are installing are safe, you should be sure to get your plugins from and, of course, read, read, read.

text:
Building a successful software platform is done by building software products that solve real problems in the ‘world-at-large’. It can’t be done behind closed doors by just a team of technical people. It has to be done with the customer.

The whole startup has to be focused on the end consumer and their unmet needs. You need to focus on those people and those unmet needs like a laser beam and everything you can think about as a startup needs to be oriented around getting those people and understanding their needs.

In terms of team-building, startups need to focus on a lot more than just the best engineers and scientists they can hire. Building a successful startup will invariably require hiring sales, user interface, and commercialization folks.

These people are just as important as the most senior technical leaders because, without them, you don’t have a product that can get in the door of the organizations that need the value you’re providing.

I believe the most successful startups are run by people who have an innate sense of what the customer needs. They are people who can articulate those needs, design and build a product that meets those needs and do it faster than anyone else.

Everyone seems to want a brighter smile, and teeth whitening is a procedure that has become very popular for that reason. It is estimated that by next year, the majority of cosmetic dental procedures will be either teeth cleaning or teeth whitening. As well as giving a person’s face a more youthful and vital appearance, it also helps to improve their self-esteem.
                                                 

The reason teeth stain

In most cases, teeth naturally lighten as we get older, but the staining process happens gradually, so there is not such a dramatic effect visible, and we do not notice it as much. However, some foods and drinks, including tea, coffee, red wine, and colored soft drinks, stain teeth more quickly. When using the services of a professional teeth whitenings dentist, the staining process can be accelerated, and we can see the results much more quickly. Sometimes stains are the result of medications such as tetracycline, and it is not possible to remove them.

How teeth whitening works

When using a teeth whitening procedure, whether it is carried out by a dentist or at home, active ingredient, hydrogen peroxide is used. This ingredient is safe for all types of teeth, and it is also safe for the gums.

Often neglected and shrugged off, wisdom teeth are a dental concern that should be taken seriously. Wisdom teeth, also known as third molars, are the last set of molars in the mouth and usually emerge between the ages of 17 to 25. If you’re lucky, you might never experience any issues with your wisdom teeth.

However, if you’re like most people, you might experience dental health problems due to wisdom teeth. At the first signs of discomfort, it’s crucial to visit an experienced dentist in Wollongong so they can assess the situation and determine if your wisdom teeth need to be extracted.

Here are some dangers of ignoring a wisdom tooth:

1. Risk of infection

The most frequent issue linked to impacted wisdom teeth is infection. As wisdom teeth attempt to emerge, they can create a haven for bacteria, leading to an infection in the surrounding gum tissue. This type of infection, known as pericoronitis, typically causes warning signs such as swelling, redness, pain, fever, bad breath, and a pus

text:
I grew up with stove top mac and cheese. A big pot of water on high heat, elbow macaroni brought to a boil, drained, and then stirred with a generous amount of butter and shredded cheddar. That was my mac and cheese for the longest time.

I can't remember when it happened, but at some point in my 20's I graduated to using shells instead of elbow macaroni. The holes just made it that much creamier, I thought. At home I was content with the stove top method. But every time I went to my in-laws' house we would have delicious, creamy mac and cheese that could rival any restaurant in town. I wanted that recipe! My mother-in-law eventually shared it with me.

My mother-in-law's mac and cheese goes like this:

In a large saucepan melt the butter over medium heat. Add the flour and stir it into the melted butter.
Cook the mixture until it thickens, constantly stirring.
Add the milk all at once and stir, stir, stir. Once the sauce becomes creamy, add the cooked shells and cheddar. Stir until the cheese is completely melted.

That's it. Easy, right? The

text:
Last night a few of us from The Bierkraft Brew Club got together to make sourdough starter. The recipe I used was mostly from the recipe that has been floating around the internet for the last few years. It calls for 1 cup of 100% hydration flour, 1 cup of water, and yeast. You are supposed to discard half of the mixture, and then feed it equal amounts of flour and water once a day for 5 days. After 5 days you should have a starter.

This method of developing a starter works by adding just enough food to keep the yeast living in the mixture without giving them enough resources to outcompete each other. The yeast will consume some of the food and reproduce. The other organisms in the mixture that we are also feeding do the same. This creates a stable ecosystem. The yeast is always the most fit for our needs because the acidic environment we have created is detrimental to most other organisms, but it is fine for yeast to grow.

I started the starter last night about 6:30, and it was ready to bake bread with by 11:00. The only real downside to this method is that it requires a lot of active time to complete the process.

Here is a picture of my starter:

text:
The High brightness is being compared to 350 Watts conventional, not 350 Watts high brightness.  So this would be around the equivalent of 500 Watts of incandescent light.  That’s still a lot of light, but the high brightness bulb actually uses less power than the low brightness bulb!
That’s the other thing you really should know about CFLs: They are most efficient at their operating temperature. Most CFLs take a few minutes to warm up to their operating temperature. Therefore, they are most efficient when they have been on for a few minutes. Therefore, any timers, photo cells, or other devices that control the power to your CFLs need to be set to allow the bulbs to be powered before the room is needed.

So for a typical application, a CFL will use about a quarter to a third of the power of incandescent lights to produce the same amount of light. As a result, you should see energy savings with CFLs. Now, not everyone is pleased with the “color temperature” of CFLs, or the time it takes for them to reach full operating temperature, or other issues. However, for many applications, CFLs will provide significant energy savings.
