text:
Both the federation of states and the federal superstate of nations would function as representative democracies with elections taking place every four years to choose representatives. These representatives would be in charge of drafting and approving legislation. In the federation of states, the central legislative, executive, and judicial bodies would be located in each member state. The federation would also have the ability to create a central executive and legislative body for when it needs to coordinate between the states.

                   

As for the federal superstate of nations, legislative bodies would be located in each nation. These would be joined by a central legislative, executive, and judicial body responsible for managing the federal superstate of national entities overall.

In both cases, this system allows for both local decision-making within the member states or member nations and overall governance to tackle pan-regional issues. This way, the right balance between autonomy and unity is maintained in both structures.

Benefits of a Federated State Union and a Federal Superstate of Nations

The benefit of this dual governance structure is that it allows for better cooperation between states and nations without the need to give up too much power. In practice, it would mean each member state or nation would maintain a fair degree of autonomy,

In this paper, we will review the basics of nanoparticles with an
emphasis on synthesis, processing, commercialization, application(s)

Bridging science and policy decision-making.

The aim

Bridging science and policy decision making to improve our society and environment.

Fostering a

Culture that enables scientists and policy makers to effectively communicate uncertainty, risk, and knowledge.

Our focus

Flamingos! What? Why Flamingos? As a biologist, one of the most valuable lessons that field biologists learn is that nature is complex, non-linear, and often surprising. Flamingos are no exception, they are social animals, living in massive, tightly knit flocks. To navigate, birds use visual cues and maintain a certain distance from their neighbours. When one bird changes direction, all quickly do the same. 3.     Price the property:  The listing price must be fair and realistic, based on a comparative market analysis and any applicable short sale guidelines.

text:
Justice studies is an interdisciplinary area that blends topics from several areas such as Law, Sociology, Political Science, Social Psychology, History, etc. These social sciences seek to understand the characteristics of the current legal system and the effects, positive or negative, of the policies and procedures related to social control in the modern societies. At the core of the field of Justice studies rests the aim of making justice a more fair, accessible, transparent and efficient institution.

The objectives of this area are:

  1. Analyze and evaluate the different models that exist in the current institutionalized forms of justice.
  2. Contribute to the improvement of the institutional mechanisms of the various jurisdictions (legal, administrative, etc.).
  3. Develop legal and social policies for the promotion of social transformation and the creation of justice.
  4. Study the impact of social and economic dynamics on different fields of the administrative and criminal justice.

To achieve these objectives, Justice Studies professionals develop the skills in critical and creative analysis, research, critical thinking and communication necessary to work on issues of public policy, institutional efficiency, social transformation and development.Sunday, August 14, 2011

text:
Is downloading from torrent sites illegal in the USA?
Oct 10, 2011

For the record my torrent usage is pretty low as I just use it to download indie games.

So I figured I’d see what the legal status of downloading from torrent sites is in the USA. Also what the punishment for downloading something copyrighted would be?

You can download stuff copyrighted by others, you just can’t upload or seed it. Only problem is that it is rather difficult to download something without uploading.

Torrents themselves are not illegal, so downloading anything from a torrent site is not necessarily illegal – but you are still very likely to be breaking copyright law. It’s a difficult area to navigate, and as such it might well be simpler to just avoid downloading or sharing copyrighted material (since that’s clearly not legal).

The punishment varies by jurisdiction and the exact nature of what has been pirated. You can potentially be hit with heavy fines and/or hit with a lawsuit, but it’s very unlikely that you will be taken to court over small stuff, although it has happened before.

Downloading stuff from websites like pirate bay is illegal. If you are caught torrenting something can range from warning letters to lawsuits
prompt_prefix: Is downloading from torrent sites illegal in the USA?

By Cecile Green and Rebecca Fisher-McGinty, Ph.D.

The concept of mindfulness, once a term associated almost entirely with eastern meditation practices, has become a buzzword in modern language over the the past few decades. Now, mindfulness seems to be everywhere.

“Be mindful,” we hear time and time again, but what does that mean? Can mindfulness really help when it comes to anxiety, stress, and other troubles? And is mindfulness something that you can learn?

The answer is a qualified “yes” to all of the above. Mindfulness is a deliberate mental state in which you focus on the present moment, and it might be something you can learn and practice. While research into mindfulness is ongoing, it has already yielded important ways that mindfulness impacts our brains and how we interact with the world around us.

At its most basic level, mindfulness means paying attention to the present moment. It might be the way your feet feel on the ground while you stand, or the way your breath feels as you inhale and exhale.

Mindfulness is not, of course, simply paying attention to the present. There are several key aspects of mindfulness that are important to keep in mind. First

"It came down to who wanted it more," Haynes said. "We wanted it more."
It wasn't pretty by any means, but it's progress. The defense that was once terrible now can consider themselves at least average.
Head coach Art Briles expressed his excitement about where the defense has come.
"We've come a long ways as a football team. I'm proud of them right now," said Briles. "They're making plays, they're tackling well, they're keeping us in the games. We still need to continue to improve, but right now we're playing with a lot of confidence."

text:
Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD)

What is Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD)?

Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a progressive decrease in kidney function which can be caused by Diabetes and Hypertension. CKD leads to progressive destruction of the kidney which can eventually lead to End Stage Renal Disease (ESRD).

Risk Factors of CKD

  1. Diabetes
  2. Hypertension
  3. Glomerulonephritis
  4. Polycystic Kidney Disease
  5. Interstitial Nephritis

Signs and Symptoms of CKD

  1. Fatigue
  2. Poor appetite
  3. Swelling in the legs, feet, hands, face
  4. Sleep problems
  5. Anemia

Complications of CKD

  1. Anemia
  2. Bone and muscle problems
  3. High blood pressure

Prevention of CKD

  1. Control of blood pressure with medications and regular exercise
  2. Monitoring and control of blood glucose in Diabetes
  3. Dietary modifications with limitation of high potassium, high phosphorus, and low protein diet
  4. Checking of blood urea
prompt_prefix: Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD)

The design of the wine room was created utilizing a specific set of technologies which allow it to maintain a constant interior temperature, humidity levels, and also block out ambient sounds. This is especially important as temperature, light, and humidity all have an impact on the aging and tasting process of wine.
findViewById Properties of a Perfect Wine Room

The following are the most important requirements of a wine room design:

1. Temperature: The wine should be kept at a reasonable temperature between 13 to 20°C.
2. Light: Sunlight can cause wine to age prematurely or turn to vinegar. Wine should be stored in complete darkness.
3. Humidity: The humidity should be maintained in the range of 50 to 60 percent. High humidity levels will prevent the cork from drying out and allowing air in, while low humidity levels will cause the corks to dry out and allow air in which can spoil the wine.
4. Protection from Vibrations: Vibration can cause the sediments in the wine to be mixed which can spoil its flavor.
5. Air Circulation: Proper air circulation is required to maintain evenly the room temperature.
6. Aesthetics: A design with fine aesthetics which blends well with the rest of the

Negative Pneumatic Toothed Clutch with Double Inverted Toothed Pins

This type of clutch can transmit torque efficiently even at low pressure because the pressure is applied to both sides of the outer plate, thereby effectively increasing the bearing surface. However, because of the long lever arms of the double inverted toothed pins, the transmitted torque is more susceptible to being reversed by torque reversal of the output shaft. To minimize the effects of torque reversal, this type of clutch is generally used for intermittent and multi-directional operations, not cyclic operations.

The Linux kernel now includes everything that is needed to run it on the system, but the operating system needs some extra components to complete the system like,

The file system, which organizes the files in a logical structure.

A package manager, which is a tool that manages the installation and removal of packages or software.

A graphical user interface (GUI) and shell.

A desktop environment, which includes a window management system and a set of tools to interact with the computer.

What is a distribution in Linux?

A Linux distribution is a version or distribution of the Linux operating system, customized by a community of developers, a commercial organization, or a vendor that is maintained, developed, and distributed along with additional software packages beyond the Linux kernel.

The software packages in a Linux distribution consist of the base system along with extra utility programs that run the operating system, such as a desktop environment, text editor, web browser, and other tools. These components may come from many different sources and can include original works from individual software developers and organizations from all over the world.

A Linux distribution can be categorized into server, desktop, and mobile distributions:

With Mount Rainier only 25 miles to the southeast at the moment, we thought Mount Hood might be a good escape plan. We headed south toward Gresham along Interstate 5 past Timbers Lodge. Parked at the lodge were motorhomes, campers, pickup trucks and cars, a gathering of the faithful. We grabbed a hot dog and coke at the Timbers Mountain Express Diner, watched the live feed and decided it was getting too close for comfort.
 Commissie to the Washington office of the National Park Service.

We turned around and headed east on Interstate 5 as snow began to swirl over the freeway. There was no way we could climb Mount Hood in a driving storm this bad, never mind Mount Rainier. An hour later, we exited the freeway in Lacey, a tidy town north of Tacoma with the silhouette of Mount Rainier looming over its downtown.

Our friends from the Portland area often grouse about Seattle's gray skies and rain, but Lacey's a much drier, sunnier place. The Lewis River winds through town, and in the spring its banks are littered with colorful wildflowers.

“The key to change … is to focus all of our energy, not on fighting the deepest part of ourselves … but on the spaces between the cells, the gaps between our habits, the areas where we have not yet formed a fixed point of view.”

– Gay Hendricks

This quote struck home with me at a crucial time and in a way that opened my eyes to a much deeper concept.

It’s about change and why it’s so important. Change is one of those things many of us talk about, fewer of us think about, and even fewer implement.

If you know me, you know I’m a believer in WBS, WIS and WIFM which are the fundamental components of change:

WBS (Whole Business Scorecard) – getting all aspects of a business working together – one team.

WIS (Why is it Significant) – understanding the deeper meaning behind why what we’re doing is important and why it matters.

WIFM (What’s In it For Me) – the personal benefit for the individual.

If you are an American who is thinking about getting married to a non-US citizen, you should know about fiance(e) and marriage visas. The K1 fiance visa allows US citizens to bring their foreign fiance to the United States so they can get married.

Fiance(e) Visas

Fiance(e) visas allow US Citizens to bring their foreign fiance(e) to the United States so they can get married. A marriage visa allows a foreign national, who is currently living abroad, to come to the United States for the purpose of getting married.

The Process

The fiance visa process begins when the US Citizen Petitioner (you) petitions for his or her fiance. Once the petition is approved, the US Citizen Petitioner must pay the immigrant visa fee and the fiance visa fee. After the USCIS Approves the I-129F, the Petitioner must then send a copy of the I-129F to the National Visa Center (NVC). The NVC will send the Petition to the U.S.Consulate in the place of Birth of the Beneficiary.

Getting the Birth Certificate

Our teams of fire investigation experts at OTTE have often been called upon to investigate cases of emergencies at petrol filling stations. In order to ensure the safety of their users but also to limit the financial losses, stations must understand the causes and consequences of such events. Our expertise helps fuel retailers, insurance companies, equipment manufacturers and authorities to identify the causes of the incident, assess the extent of the damage, and prevent similar incidents from happening in future.

We combine our detailed technical knowledge of fuel systems and fire detection equipment with a thorough understanding of legal concepts so that we can identify the responsible party when necessary. text:
The Western Shoshone call themselves Newe, or "the people." They are part of a larger group of Numic Native Americans that include the Nevada Shoshone, Goshute, Paiute, and Mono tribes. Historians don't know the Western Shoshone's original homeland, but they believe it was in the area of Alaska, because they share linguistic and cultural traits with other Native Americans in that region.
 jäsen are not eligible for services.

In the 19th century, Western Shoshone traditional livelihoods were disrupted by the influx of mining, ranching, and other industries. Many Western Shoshone died as a result of new diseases, and others were forced to move to designated reservations or were pushed into the workforce to support Anglo-Americans.

Western Shoshone today maintain strong connections with their traditional ways of life. Those on the Ruby Valley Reservation often work as ranchers or miners. Western Shoshone who live off the reservation in California, Nevada, Oregon, and Idaho may work in lumber or mining industries.


Western Shoshone culture has been shaped by their close connection to the land. They traditionally divided their territory into natural regions and named important features like caves, springs, and mountain peaks.

Anyone who has done a quick search on what to do if detained in a foreign country is already familiar with the ‘do not resist, do not make accusations, do not plead guilty’ approach to being arrested and detained. What they may not be familiar with is the ‘do not answer questions without legal counsel’ approach and their rights under the U.S. criminal justice system.
  
 

It is standard protocol for American citizens to be read their Miranda rights upon arrest, which include the rights to remain silent, the right to an attorney, the right to have those attorneys present during questioning and the right to have those rights explained if the person is not a U.S. citizen. While Miranda rights are a cornerstone of U.S. criminal justice, what many people do not realize is that they can only be invoked if explicitly stated to a person upon arrest. If rights are not explicitly read this can result in a violation that may compromise the admissibility of a confession. If you are ever detained, remain silent, politely ask for an attorney and do not answer any questions until you have legal representation.

injury We use a combination of techniques for the treatment of sports injuries, which aims to treat the cause of the injury and improve the body's natural healing processes. Our sports injury treatments include physiotherapy and osteopathy.
Sports injury

Sports injuries are generally classified as acute injuries or chronic overuse injuries.
Acute injuries occur suddenly (for example, a twisted ankle or a torn muscle) and are often the result of a specific event (for example, a fall during a game). These injuries usually cause pain, swelling, and other signs of injury (for example, bruising).
Chronic overuse injuries are usually caused by repetitive or prolonged stress on the body (for example, repetitive stress to the knees during repeated jumping, or repeated tension to tendons and muscles from rowing).

Common sports injuries include:
- Sprains and strains
- Contusions (bruises)
- Tendinitis
- Bursitis
- Shin splints
- Stress responses
- Stress fractures of the legs and feet (e.g. shin splints)
- Bruising
- Strains and sprains of the shoulder
- Dislocations of the shoulder
- Fractures in the upper limbs
- Back injuries

The worlds of ancient finance and cryptocurrency have continually been at odds over the philosophical and legal implications of decentralized digital money. In this context, one is often led to consider the idea that in order to better understand and appraise the potential consequences of cryptocurrency adoption, we can seek counsel from the history of both finance and governance, to the ancient civilizations that gave them birth.
Appellees in the cryptocurrency debate have long claimed parallels between the invention of coinage in Ancient Greece and the origins of Bitcoin. As the story goes, the rise of Athenian democracy in the 6th century was facilitated by the introduction of “drachmas,” a standard silver currency that eliminated the need for patrons and clients to engage in cumbersome time-consuming barter. By quantifying value in a new “digital” currency, the ancient Athenians were also said to have established the bedrock for the kind of market transparency and regulatory accountability now espoused by “libertarian-minded” crypto-proponents. As one blockchain industry commentator remarked, the drachma “transformed Athens from a ‘client state’ into what became the world’s first modern democracy.”

text:
SRID is a tedious and time consuming assay to measure lipid rafts

LipID is an analytical method for measuring lipid rafts in cell membranes.

LipID works on the request or with a constant flow of single cells/tissues and is fully automated in a closed liquid system (biopsy-to-data) under physiological assay conditions.

LipID is fast, reproducible and reliable with a sensitivity to detect lipid rafts of ≤ 2% of the population.

Using LipID, lipid raft studies can now be performed at high throughput, making it an essential tool for lipid raft research in basic and clinical research.

LipID is used with cell lines and primary tissues under physiological condition. LipID analyses cells by single cell sorting using a cell sorter.


LipID enables researchers to analyze the lipid raft population in large cell populations (e.g., thousands of cells) on a single day and can discriminate lipid raft populations with minimal variation and reproducibility.

LipID is the only method that provides comprehensive data on lipid raft structure and function and can be coupled with other omics technologies (transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics) to decipher the molecular mechanisms underlying membrane raft heterogeneity.

What Is the Definition of a Personal Relationship Status Agreement?

A personal relationship status agreement defines the legal status of a person at their death. In the absence of a will, a person's relationship status is determined by state law.

Most states assume that property inherited by an unmarried partner is owned jointly. When a partner dies, the surviving partner may face difficulties in probating the estate. The law will likely consider the deceased person's assets as community property. This could result in high probate costs, lengthy court battles, and reduced inheritance for children from previous marriages.

A relationship status agreement would cover these situations. It would specify the property that is jointly owned. It would also detail how any jointly owned property will be managed upon the death of one partner. Estate planning lawyers can provide advice on the best way to proceed. In most cases, putting property under a trusts can be effective. It ensures privacy and confidentiality. Trusts bypass the estate, so there is less probate court supervision. This prevents the surviving partner from

text:
← Mechanics of Creation 3: Super-Nova Explosions

In the 1970s, the Soviet Union launched the first space telescope in support of the Lomonosov mission to Venus. This instrument, called the Venerable 1 and 2 orbital telescope, was designed by the Skobeltsyn Institute of Nuclear Physics. The probe was to orbit the Sun between Venus and Earth and carry out a comprehensive investigation of the interstellar space that the Venera probes had entered. To this end, it was equipped with a compact optical system designed by the SKB Research Institute for the Development of Applied Mechanics headed by Lev Ilyuk, which allowed the telescope to capture on film the images of comets (using a long exposure) and the Moon and planets (with a shorter exposure, by means of a magnifying telescope). However, this first telescope suffered technical difficulties on launch and failed to enter orbit.

Soviet scientists then had to wait until 1981, when the Vega mission blasted off with two space probes, each fitted with a separate instrument for observing and photographing comets, the planets, the Kuiper Belt, the Oort Cloud, and other objects on the edge of the solar system. The probes also carried a disk of micro

The weather outside doesn't always call for a jacket. With temperatures that swing from 40 to 80 degrees in the course of a week, we thought it important to explore how to dress for the seasons. It's not the seasons, really. It's Santa Fe.
. We spoke to Santa Fe native Steve Schindler, co-owner of Barefoot Bob's Trademark clothing store since 1986, about what to wear during the changing seasons.

The change from summer to fall

You can wear a lighter chambray, gingham or seersucker weight fabric, something with texture, whereas before [summer] it was strictly white. You can wear a darker shirt, or a brighter one like a citrus yellow or orange. You can also start introducing earth tones, like an olive chambray shirt. I'm not saying that you can't wear earth tones in the summer, but you can start warming it up.

The change from fall to winter

At the end of October, I start wearing my wool flannel, which is two layers of wool flannel [stitched] together. It's a full flannel but not as thick as overcoat flannel. I can even wear it in the 70s,

The fish is generally non-aggressive, and does not pose a threat to the "normal" fish keeping inhabitants of an aquarium. The fish is quite peaceful and can be kept with most other fish.


The Knight goby is a very hardy and robust fish, and can therefore tolerate somewhat low water quality and poor environmental conditions.

The Knight goby is a very intelligent fish and seems to enjoy challenges. It is therefore a good idea to include caves and rock work in the aquarium, to keep the Knight goby happy and entertained. It is also reported that the Knight goby has problem recognizing and accepting mirror image decorations as real ones, and it can therefore get confused and distressed. If this happens, try to remove the mirror from the aquarium.

Sexing the Knight Goby

You can usually tell the male Knight goby apart from the female by looking at the dorsal fin. In males, the dorsal fin is more triangular in shape. Males also have darker, pointed markings along either side of the body, whilst females have a broader, less distinct stripe.

To measure a dumpster, you measure its length, width, and height in feet and inches. Information Security Risk Awareness Programs in K-12 Education

The importance of information security has grown since the 1972 break-in at the Democratic National Committee headquartered in the Watergate office complex where five men were arrested for the bugging and wiretapping of phones. The United States has witnessed various cybercrimes, such as the 2016 United States presidential election interference. Cybercrimes in education have also occurred in the last few years. In 2020, the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) notified over 80,000 students that their personal data was compromised. UCLA was using a third-party vendor, and the breach started in October 2017. It’s not just universities that suffered but also some high schools. In 2019, the Houston Independent School District announced that the personal information of 300,000 students and employees was breached. The information included social security numbers, addresses, and driver's license numbers.

These cybercrimes remind us of the importance of information security and the need for security risk awareness practices in the education field. This study investigates the components and best practices for information security risk awareness practices that should be incorporated into K-12 education curriculum. K-12 education is elementary and high school education that provides basic understanding and secondary education

The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) recently proposed changes to the government’s procurement framework in the United States, specifically with its Request for Proposals (RFP). The idea is to promote better cybersecurity measures, increased privacy controls, stronger government procurement processes, and to reduce vendor overlap.

The changes would include more specific criteria on how agencies should approach contracts for cloud storage, cybersecurity, and data privacy. In addition, agencies would need to look at how to ensure a more diverse vendor list when issuing RFPs.

If proposed, vendors selected for contracts above a $25 million threshold would be subject to a review by an independent assessment firm to ensure they meet security obligations.

Applications for the 2019 Fellowship are now closed. Applications for the 2020 Fellowship will open in late 2018.

The Radcliffe Fellowship in the Humanities is a prestigious and competitive grant for scholars at the beginning or middle of their careers. Over the past decade, Radcliffe Fellows have used their awardsto fund research projects on every continent, from the prehistoric to the present day, and to compose a wide variety of cultural genres, from histories to novels to art exhibitions. In every case, the work they have produced has addressed the most complex and important questions about the nature of human societies.

Past awards have supported projects in a vast range of fields, from anthropology and archaeology to history, literature, and the visual arts, and from the study of specific societies and time periods to much broader comparative enquiries. The one requirement is that the work address one or more of the questions that matter most to the humanities today: what is the relationship between the past and the present, and how can it be best investigated? How can we best understand the multiple characteristics that define individuals, including their membership in particular groups? Copies Source file to Dest file, preserving modification times and file flags. If Dest already exists, it may not be overwritten depending on your OS's settings.
mkfile size filename
Creates an empty file of specified size.
mklost size
Creates a lost file of specified size.
mkdir dir
Creates a directory.
move file ... dir
Moves files/directories from Source to Dest, overwriting if Dest already exists.
mprint [-fNnVv] [file ...]
Prints files in the usual way, but also handles printing Fortran IV source code, Pascal source code, and files which are directories.
msort [-fLlLRNnSp] [file...]
Sort and/or merge files. The options are similar to nprint and dprint, but -f shows the original file locations, and -l sorts lexicographically case-insensitively, -lL shows differences line by line, and -L displays identical lines after differences.
nprint [file ...]
Print files, with various formatting/letter casing options.
pbcopy SrcDst
Backup first copy of Src to Dst, then copy subsequent copies

Police Brutality - ACTS Law Firm in Los Angeles CA

Police Brutality

Police brutality is a term that refers to excessive police force or violence used against a person. Police brutality can take many forms and can range from verbal abuse to physical injuries, such as from punches, kicks, electric shock, tasers, and other weapons. Although police officers are necessary for maintaining law and order in society, it is not an excuse for them to intentionally harm a person while attempting to enforce the law.

The excessive use of force by police officers often goes unchecked due to the powerful position they occupy within the justice system. If you are a victim of police brutality, you may have grounds for a lawsuit and need the help of an experienced police brutality attorney.

Types of Police Brutality & Civil Rights Violations

Police brutality can take many forms, and the actions of police officers in certain situations may result in civil rights violations. Civil rights are the rights of a person that ensure equal protection under the law and the right to liberty, life, and property. Some of the civil rights that are most commonly violated by police officers include:

  • The Fourth Amendment Right to Freedom from Unreasonable Searches and Seizures: The

Peculiarities of the 2006 Census

Yesterday's Age reported on some of the more unusual answers provided by Australians to the August 2006 Census. While I understand that these types of articles provide light relief and a bit of a chuckle during Census season, I must challenge the tone of the article.

When the reporter asked me for comment, I was somewhat dismayed at the focus of their approach. There seemed to be an expectation that people who submitted unusual responses were somehow eccentric or even deviant.

I provided the reporter with a balanced perspective: noting that the Census has always attracted a range of responses, and that more unusual responses are particularly visible given that every response is now electronically linked and searchable on the ABS website.

I pointed out that, while the majority of unusual responses are light-hearted and may be dismissed as mere pranks or jokes, others reveal real concerns about the way the census data is used in policy-making. text:
A past newsletter featured the amazing transformation of the Potala Palace.

The Palace was the most imposing structure in Lhasa, Tibet. It was built by the King Songtsen Gampo in 630 AD and was added to over the centuries. It became the primary residence of the Dalai Lama beginning in the 14th century.

The Potala Palace was over 500 feet long, 370-feet deep, and towered over 15,000 feet above the city.

Over 1,000 monks lived in the palace, and there were over 9,000 rooms in the 1,000-room main building. The palace included the throne room, assembly hall, assembly hall, assembly hall, and the throne room of the Dalai Lama.

It was a powerful symbol of Tibetan culture and history.

What happened next shocked everyone.

The Cultural Revolution swept across China like a tsunami. It was a period of intense violence, widespread social upheaval, and severe disruption of China's economy.

In 1974, the Red Guards entered Lhasa and occupied the Potala Palace. They turned it into a museum.

It would never again be the glorious center of Tibetan Buddhism.

There's no exposition or backstory, there's no lore to uncover or side-quests to complete, you simply know that you must find a way to reach the Titan and slay it with your small, but deadly sword.

Thankfully, this minimalist narrative direction extends to the game's world and design. Every area is a beautifully hand-drawn, interconnected hub of floating islands connected by narrow wooden walkways. text:
Custom Ribbon | Ribbon And Bows Oh My

A friend of mine wanted to make her own ribbon so she can have it for her stuff.
Here is how she did it.

Gather the moose ribbon, cardstock, bone folder, bone folder covered in wax, hole punch and tape.

She took a piece of the moose ribbon, folded it in half (so the two short ends were together), and taped it in the middle.

Next, she wrapped the bone folder in wax (it will make it easier to score through the cardstock), and using the bone folder (covered in wax) she scored through the cardstock. She only needed to go through the cardstock once.

After she scored through the cardstock it created a small crease.

Then she used the hole punch to open up the crease. Tripp Lite's NetController 8-port Gigabit switch
(Click for larger image)

Tripp Lite today announced its first home networking product: a Gigabit switch with eight 10/100 and four Gigabit ports. The design of the Tripp Lite NetController 8-port Gigabit switch addresses potential power and device compatibility issues users often encounter with home networks that may not be apparent when simply comparing features and price.

Devices can easily draw twice or three times the power as indicated on a wall outlet. Overloading a switch with too many high-power devices can drop voltage and cause malfunctions. To combat this, the NetController features Tripp Lite's patented Power Management System that automatically prevents a device from turning on if it exceeds the total power capacity of the switch.

The NetController also features a patented surge protection system to protect against short circuits and voltage spikes. Tripp Lite states that some surge protectors on other switches on the market may interfere with the electronics of HomePlug AV adapters, which use the home's wiring as a network. But the NetController is designed to prevent this from happening.

Though its features

Octopuses are invertebrates that have three hearts and blue blood, and they are known for their incredible intelligence. If the’re successful in mating with a female, male octopuses often die shortly after as a result of exhaustion. Because of this, octopus populations rely on natural selection to maintain their numbers.

A previous attempt to breed octopuses in captivity failed in 1996, and scientists believe the lack of success has been due to the inability to create the right conditions in artificial insemination.

Would you ever try to persuade an octopus to mate in a laboratory? Let us know in the comments!

Friday, 3 April 2015

Why the "Rape Jihad" Panic Is Wrong

A recent media-fueled panic about "Rape Jihad" in Europe is premised on a series of myths and misunderstandings, and misrepresents the nature of the problem posed by Muslim immigrant communities in Europe.

First, it is inaccurate to say that most sexual assault convictions in many European countries are of Muslims or immigrant origin. This is not true of e.g. France, the UK, Denmark, Sweden or Norway, at least according to official government statistics. It is true of some countries, such as some of the smaller Northern countries like Sweden and Denmark, that a relatively large proportion of sexual assault convictions in some recent years were of immigrant origin, but there are a number of reasons for this that are not related to "Jihad" or Islamism:

*Many immigrants are young men who are more likely to commit sexual assault.
*Police practices regarding immigration status and rape have changed radically in recent years, meaning that more immigrants are being prosecuted and convicted

Larry checks in with KPCC reporter Sharon McNary. What did the search teams hope to find this morning?

McNary: They were hoping to re-discover the locations of the bodies where they had left them yesterday and also to continue digging outside the house where the body of the wife was found. They have found some remains in the house they're calling possible human remains, but there isn't much to go on because they say the house was so full of clutter it's hard to tell much.

Larry: So is this clutter a hindrance to investigators?

McNary: Yes it is. The county coroner had to request a judge's order basically to get the homeowner here to at least clear out the house so they have room to breathe and work on the scene. That order says the property owner has to supply housing and meals to the investigators, the team, and remove the clutter from the house. When I talked to the coroner, he said the clutter was the biggest delay, that they could handle it, and it hadn't hindered their work too much, except for the house not being cleared. Sometimes it is the things you see every day which are most worth looking at. Take, for example, a deck of playing cards. At first, it all seems quite random, with the red and black suits; the numbering on the cards; the four suits of thirteen cards each with all the possible values (two up to ten and then some face cards). At first it might seem too random. Once we understand the rules of the game, though, all of this seems less random and more of a structure that is both necessary and well-suited to the playing of the game. Who are we… REALLY?

By: Sami Rahal

There’s a question that haunts us every day and one that we don’t want to answer honestly: Who are we, really? I mean beyond our jobs or titles; who are we, truly?

When someone tells us their job or what they “do”, we quickly categorize them and then decide our next actions, if we will interact with them, or if we will allow them to be part of our “circle” (our group of people that we feel closely connected).

We think we know them based on their job, based on a label. We are quick in judging people and categorizing them as successful, happy, unfulfilled, smart, or rich; we’re good at that.

But what if we judged our friends based on what they read, the actions they take in their communities, or what they volunteer for? What if we valued the conversations that we have with them, their character, loyalty, and sense of humor?

It’s rare that we think of these questions, we are so consumed by our own lives that we focus on getting that

Have you ever felt like your senses are totally overwhelmed? You might be able to blame your tech-obsessed kids, the constant blaring of the TV, and the endless notifications on your devices. Sensory overload is basically an excess of sensory input. There are two kinds of sensory overload. Sensory Modulation Disorder in which individuals are unable to properly adapt their sensory responses to the environment and Sensory Processing Disorder in which the brain and nervous system are unable to process all of the sensory information it receives.


In children, the symptoms of sensory overload are:

  • Difficulty paying attention
  • Excessive and seemingly purposeless behaviour
  • Emotional instability
  • Inability to transition from one activity to another
  • Difficulty following directions and understanding concepts that are related to time and space
  • Sensitivity to the tags on their clothes, textures in their foods, or lights on their skin

text:
Caused by pituitary adenomas, ischemic necrosis, craniopharyngioma, and metastases.
SymptomsMay present with coexistence of gonadotropin deficiency and growth hormone deficiency.
TreatmentMay necessitate neurosurgical intervention.

Tumors of the hypothalamus or nearby structures are responsible for approximately 40% of all nonpituitary endocrine tumors. Because of their proximity to the sellar structure, they often compress the third ventricle, resulting in hydrocephalus. Symptoms related to tumor-related compression may also include headache, visual disturbances, and bitemporal hemianopsia.

Posterior Pituitary Tumors

Posterior pituitary tumors are exceedingly rare and include neuroblastoma, adenosis, and gangliocytomas. Despite their size, most tumors of the posterior lobe do not encroach upon the third ventricle. Because of the close anatomical connection between the pituitary gland and the hypothalamus, tumors involving surrounding structures (e.g., the lamina terminalis, mammillary bodies) may also be classified as pituitary adenomas. Posterior pituitary tumors typically present with decreased secretion of growth hormone, thyroid-stimulating hormone, and adrenocorticotropic hormone (and therefore hypopituitarism may be a presenting sign).

This section provides a tutorial example on how to call a remote service for a simple use case (fetching current weather temperature), and how to use the 'RemoteRequest' class to do it in a more structured way.
PtrFromString is the header file that defines the type of the returned pointer to be sent on the wire.
The basic way to use the RemoteRequest class:
The RemoteRequest class is used to request data from a Remoting object. You can use the Get() method of the RemoteRequest to request any data. After sending the request, call GetResponse() to check if there is a response. If there is a response, call GetData().
How to get the request context in remote request and request header:
If you need to get the request context and request header in remote request processing, you can use the Context() method.
The Context() method returns a context object. The context object has a pointer to the request object and request object has the request header and some other useful information.
How to do an asynchronous request and how to send data:
If you receive a request to a handler that requires sending some data back to the client, you need to send the data back to the client before returning from the handler.

It's safe to say that the past two weeks have been an emotional and difficult time for me.

I will be honest with you, one of my biggest fears came true. I was afraid I would be judged. A person would hear the story and instead of showing compassion would instead show hatred and judgement. That happened, it was hard to swallow, but I knew it would happen.

I have also been overwhelmed by the compassion, love and support from soooo many of you. I will forever be grateful for the outpouring! It has been a blessing to read your comments and messages. To know that there are such caring, loving people in this world fills my heart with both gratitude and love.

I had planned on writing a follow up post on what happened and how we are doing now. But in all honesty, I'm just not feeling up to it. Not tonight anyway.

Instead, I will leave you with a picture of a sunrise from the coast in Oregon. There has been a lot of darkness in my mind lately and seeing this sunrise has helped to bring some peace.

Tomorrow we will talk more about what happened to me.

Dig and Rig Equipment Dedicated to the HDD Process

HDD equipment suppliers offer a full range of tools and machines necessary to complete every aspect of horizontal directional drilling. HDD machines range from relatively small trenchless drilling machines that are pulled by a skid steer to large HDD drill rigs. HDD equipment suppliers, like Sandiacre, offer equipment such as HDD reamers and other dedicated equipment for specific phases of the HDD process.

HDD Reamers

The first step in horizontal directional drilling is creating a trenchless hole with minimal impact on the surrounding soil and structures. This involves a variety of specialized HDD equipment such as horizontal directional drill (HDD) machines, tunnel boring machines (TBMs or Tunnel Boring Machines), and directional drills.

Directional drills are used for drilling through various substances, such as ice, and are controlled by a skid steer to operate efficiently. TBMs are larger, highly complex machines used for creating underground tunnels for transportation, oil and gas pipelines, telecommunications cables, and water and sewer lines.

After creating the tunnel, a steel pipe is inserted into the hole and reaming is required to enlarge the hole to fit the pipe. HDD reamers

Personally, I like to log onto WoW when I have a chunk of time to raid, say 2-3 hours. That's plenty of time to hop into an instance, knock it out, and be on my merry way. However, 2-3 hours ends up turning into 4-5 hours easier than I'd like to admit. What I end up doing is hopping into an instance, staying until we need a tank/healer/DPS to finish the last couple of bosses, and before you know it you spend three hours in an instance you've already cleared.

That's why I'm starting to limit my instance-running to an hour each night. It's easy to kill the bosses that are easiest for my class/spec (Champs or UK right now), get some badges, and a few other bits. After that, I go out and quest or play with my bank alt.

text:
The Telemetry Distribution Board is a very useful addition to a standard Raspberry Pi. We don’t yet have many products that will directly plug in to the port, but the headers for SPI, I2C and UART allow many peripherals to be connected easily. The data from these peripherals can be monitored and logged using the RPi.GPIO package.
Programming Concepts

The examples we provide for interfacing with the GPS and Temperature are based on a few basic programming concepts. These are used throughout the software examples on this site and elsewhere.

Reading Data from Ports

Reading data from the hardware connected to the board involves reading a portion of the Raspberry Pi’s memory. The mmap() function can be used to access the hardware registers as a part of the normal memory space. This allows data to be read from the registers just like variables in Python.

Here’s a simple example from the Temperature logger:

# Get temperature from sensor
adc_value = pi_v2_temp.read_adc() & 0b11111110

This code calls a library function read_adc() which puts the result in to a memory location that’s address is the

What happened?

The "Invoice" line in that duplication shows an OTD amount of $8,649.26 instead of $8,649.23. We need to make the payment balance for this Invoice zero, and add that extra $26 to the second $8,649.23 Invoice.

How do I do that?

Click the Edit Button on that Invoice.

That opens up a box to Edit that Invoice.  The Payment Balance box (far right) should show $0.00 for that Invoice, not $8,649.26.  So, just enter $0.00 in that box.

In the Payment Amount box (second from the right), enter $8,649.26.  

Click the Change button, and the Invoice is updated. screen04b.jpeg

Copyrighted works of writing, art, or music, and the't get you sued. The first step to creating an excellent game is to make sure you're playing on legal ground.

1. Copyrighted Material

Copyrighted material includes books, stories, art, music, and other creative work. If you want to use someone else's idea, story, or art, you need to get permission. You also need to make sure you're giving original creators the appropriate credit.

2. Trademarks

Trademarks are symbols, names, or phrases used to identify companies or their products. Using a logo without permission can lead to legal trouble; however, trademark is a more complex issue. If the trademark is used in a way that leads to confusion or dilutes the trademark's value, it could be considered infringement. If you want to use a famous trademark - even if it's in a way that isn't confusing to customers - you should get permission. However, using a trademark in a limited way (for example, only in your game's title), may not be infringement. Always be careful when using trademarks.

3. Patents

Patents apply to mechanical or manufacturing processes, which are typically not used in video
prompt_prefix: Copyrighted works of writing, art, or music

A common tactic that sellers are using is having an estate agent market the property without a price and say, “If you’re interested, call us.” The estate agent can then get a number of phone numbers and get an overview of the market conditions. It's actually a simple concept: 3 sub-roles you can choose to fulfill when you play: Tank, DPS, and Heals.

Someone has to be in front of the mobs/enemies taunting and taking the blows for the team that the rest of the party will be dishing out damage to (DPS). This is usually a warrior or paladin or druid (in some games).

You want to get the enemy down quickest and easiest? This is you. DPS - damage, punishment, beating down... you get the picture. According to the study targeted on a group of 38 participants, there were several differences observed in the brain activity between people who had anxiety and people who did not. For example, the anterior insula, which is involved in processing bodily sensations and emotions, was less active in the brains of people with anxiety.

On the other hand, the anterior cingulate cortex was more active, a part of the brain associated with the fight or flight reflex. This increased activity could explain the heightened physiological reactions in people with anxiety.

These findings are a great contribution towards medical progress in treating anxiety disorders and the mental aspect of it. This proves that anxiety is not just a state of mind, but also a physical problem, thus making it even more crucial for people to not ignore signs of anxiety.

How Does A Car Alternator Work

Car alternators are usually situated behind your belt driven pulley on the engine. That's the serpentine belt. What’s wrong with this sentence? It’s a “run-on sentence” with two independent clauses or “main ideas”.  It should be broken into two sentences:  “Jane went to the department store.” “She bought everything on her list.”  Here’s another one: “It was a cold dark night with no moon out the bats came out to fly the winds was gusty and the roads was muddy.”  Again, two main ideas need to be separated.

4. Over-using adverbs and adjectives:

Adverbs tell how, when and where and are formed by adding “-ly” to most adjectives. For example, “He spoke loudly” or “He played the guitar elegantly.” While they can add color to writing, they should be used judiciously.  If they don’t add new information, take them out.

5. Using incorrect homonyms:

Their, there, they’re, two, too, to.  Affect, effect, etc.  Make sure you know the difference and use them correctly.

Why Many Businesses Choose to Incorporate in Delaware

Delaware has been a popular choice for businesses looking to incorporate or form an LLC for many years because of the 1921 Domestic Corporation Law. However, the state also offers numerous other benefits and advantages that make it an attractive option for businesses from around the world. In fact, approximately 40% of the Fortune 500 companies are incorporated and maintain their headquarters in Delaware. Here’s a comprehensive guide to some of the benefits that make Delaware a prime location for incorporation.

  1. Business-Friendly Court System

Having a strong court system that’s fair and efficient is essential in resolving disputes among businesses. In Delaware, this is achieved through the Court of Chancery, which makes judicial decisions based on business realities as opposed to legal requirements. This court is separate and distinct from the general district courts, allowing for quicker and faster resolution times. What’s more, in the event of a stalemate or disagreement on a matter within the company, the Court of Chancery is responsible for addressing and resolving the dispute.

  1. Business Confidentiality

In Delaware, corporations and LLC’s are not required to disclose their shareholders or members. This is particularly beneficial for businesses engaging in intellectual property

text:
Bitcoin is a currency, used to exchange goods and services, and yes, its price can fluctuate.


"That's the 'price' of a bitcoin," said Mike Balaam of the Texas Bitcoin Association.

Some of the fluctuation is based on supply and demand.

"Bitcoin in 2012, there were very very few, and that's why the price went up," Balaam said.

As more people started using it for purchases, the price went up. As more bitcoins are "mined," or brought into circulation, the reward for mining is reduced.

When the first person created the bitcoin application, they set it up so the number of bitcoins in circulation would never exceed 21 million.

"And now we are getting closer and closer to that number. It's 17 million now. So, as you mine for bitcoin, instead of getting 50 bitcoins you get 25. And that happens every time they half the amount," Balaam said.

Some experts say bitcoin is unregulated, making it attractive for people looking to hide money or get around tax laws - and that has some people feeling uneasy. Balaam says it's largely self-regulated, and that it is tracked

A few years ago, I was on tour with my old band, The Suburban Lawns, doing a show outside of Chicago. In the winter. As the sun was setting. I remember standing on the stage, shivering in the cold, realizing that this was probably not why I got into music.

That night, a new hero was born - Eric "Seasick Steve" Matthews. If you haven't heard of him, or seen any videos of him, you owe it to yourself to check him out. A wild-haired, one-string-guitar playing, hobo-roaming troubadour, who has a style that's as earthy and as soulful as it gets.

In the two years since our first encounter, Seasick Steve has seen a bit of fame, playing last year's Glastonbury, making appearances on David Letterman, and even appearing on the cover of Guitar Player magazine.

This past weekend, Seasick Steve made his way to the Finger Lakes, playing a show as part of the Elizabethtown music festival.

"Wrong. Try me", Lana says to Malcolm in the February 15 episode of "Archer". But what happens when you put these two espionage professionals in the same bar, where one is drinking and the other is undercover? Mayhem, of course.

Irate at Lana for not giving him credit for cracking a drug case, Malcolm has decided to give her no peace until their accounting dispute is cleared up. So he stakes out the ISIS cocktail party, fully intending to corner her into thanking him.

But Lana is prepared for him. She has set the bar well in advance with one stipulation -- no talking. This means, of course, that there will be no way for Malcolm to get his due in public. So instead of going into full-on jerk mode, Malcolm tries to subtly win Lana over when they wind up in the same bar.

NSF file stands for Notes shared file.

Yes, It is possible to share a single data source among multiple databases.

It depends upon the platform hardware , OS and total available disk space.

Magnetic brain waves reveal memory storage

Electroencephalogram brain scan

Researchers have discovered how neurons in the brain communicate through magnetic brain waves to help create and recall long-term memories(Source: iStockphoto)

Scientists say they have uncovered the secret of how the brain uses magnetic brain waves to store and recall long-term memories.

The discovery, published in the journal Neuron , shows that neurons communicate with one another to record and then retrieve memories of events and facts.

"The prevailing idea has been that these magnetic brain waves create spatial patterns of activity in the brain that encode information. The new study shows that the communication between neurons is through these magnetic brain waves," says Jim Ranck from the University of California, Berkeley.

For the study, researchers exposed rabbits to tones of two different frequencies at the same time. The animals were able to learn to distinguish the two pitch patterns and react differently when they heard the two combinations.

IO::CompfiXXuUxSS(3pm)


# NAME

IO::CompfiXXuUxSS -- Terminal IO via SSH tunneling and OOB

# DESCRIPTION

IO::CompfiXXuUxSS provides an abstraction to redirect terminal IO to a remote server across SSH tunnel.

IO::CompfiXXuUxSS provides an abstraction of theIO::UnixIOvia the ssh connection, IO::CompfiXXuUxSS supports onlySTDOUT,STDERRandSTDINon the local machine.

The remote side of the connection must be started with the IO::CompfiXXuUxSS::Server. The server will accept a given port and forward all the data from that port across the established SSH connection to the local machine.

IO::CompfiXXuUxSS::Consumer runs on the local machine and establish a tunnel to remote server using ssh. It feeds all the output to the local side of that tunnel and reads from other side

Well, this really depends on the team. In Ubers you have so much raw power, that a Terrakion boasting an Attack of 676 can get quite overshadowed. You might want to invest in some Speed in order to outspeed some threats. The best option would be the one you listed - Timid Nature with 252 SpAtk, 252 Spe, and 4 Atk EVs. However if your team is pretty slow, that's fine. With that being said, there are a few other variations of that set that you can run.

252 Atk / 4 Def / 252 Spd Nature - This gives Terrakion the most Attack possible, along with decent Special Defense and decent Speed.

Adamant / Jolly Nature with 252 HP, 252 Atk, and 6 SpD / 4 Def EVs - This would allow Terrakion the most physical bulk and a pretty decent HP stat.

Timid / Naive Nature with 4 HP,

Viniferia: Quality Italian Wines


Brunello di Montalcino

Brunello di Montalcino

Brunello di Montalcino is an Italian red wine produced in the Montalcino region of Tuscany, which lies between the towns of Florence and Siena. The wine is named for the eponymous grape variety, Brunello di Montalcino. Brunello di Montalcino is characterized by its deep ruby color, aromas of sweet red and black fruit, and complex tannins. Brunello is designed to mature for many years. The wine cannot be sold until five years after harvest and is often aged for fifteen or twenty years. Montalcino is unique among Tuscan DOCs in encouraging long-term aging. Montalcino's volcanic soil, warm climate, and the clone of Sangiovese known as prugnolo gentile contribute to the wine's complexity and longevity.

All wines marked with (*) are organically produced.

Tasting Notes

The combination of Sangiovese grape and the volcanic red clay and marl soil in Montalcino produce a wine with a high tannin

 Virtual Network Peering
Virtual Network (VNet) Peering allows you to connect two or more Microsoft Azure virtual networks so that virtual machines (VMs) in each VNet can communicate with each other as if they were in the same IP address space. By using VNet Peering, you can reduce the costs associated with transferring data between the VNets, while improving latency by enabling direct connectivity between resources in separate VNets.

In Azure, when you deploy resources to the cloud, they are typically deployed to a single VNet. However, there may be scenarios where you want to deploy resources to different VNets either for enhanced network isolation, regulatory requirements, or security reasons. VNet Peering enables you to connect these resources in different VNets and allows them to effectively communicate with each other as if they were in the same VNet.

VNet Peering can be used for various scenarios, such as transferring workloads from one VNet to another without changing IP address ranges, connecting two or more VNets together for workloads that require direct communication between them (for example, an analytics cluster), extending a VNet with additional

It is generally easier for people who grew up playing video games to transition to a dancepad for a few reasons. First, they're used to the 'feel' of a device with buttons you have to press quickly and in sequence. Second, they know how to focus intently on a task and react quickly when necessary, both important skills for playing DDR. Finally, they are used to seeing and following directions on a TV screen.

But anyone can learn to play! DDR is, at its core, a game, and like all games, everyone can get better with a little practice. Even if you've never played video games before, you can still learn to play DDR; you'll just have to work a little harder at remembering the steps. Since you don't have any bad habits to unlearn, you can learn the most basic steps and then work your way up. Your instructor will be happy to help you learn and can even recommend a few songs for beginners.

In the civil law there is the concept of co-signer. If you as a principal sign a contract, you are responsible for the debts as long as you live. But when that debt is assumed by another or divided between you and another under the civil law and you have a co-signer (the civil law says that: one who co-signs is obligated should the principal fail to pay), then that co-signer is obligated to pay. 3010110111020222(BASE 2) Numereral Spectrometry #011 #022# Why does the IBM Q Experience returns wrong values for Quantum Circuits running more than once?



        in the IBM Quantum Experience, after running a quantum circuits more than 1 time (with the default number of runs), it gives wrong results from my point of view. Does anyone know why this happen and how to avoid it?

A quantum computer doesn't "return" a result.

There is a computational process which, for a given quantum state and a given measurement type, generates a distribution of results. The quantum computer is run and the results are sampled. Those results are the outcome of the probabilistic distribution, and since the quantum computer will often be run many times, the results can be collated and statistical understanding can be gained about them - what the "most likely" results were and how close others were to this most likely result and exactly what the least likely result was, etc. etc.

It would be surprising if every time you ran the quantum computer with a given set of inputs you got the exact same answer. (Actually, it's more surprising that there are any answers that have a high probability of being returned.)

 Corner Column:
How Not Like Church… Why Attend One?

Why do you attend a church?

I’ve asked that question of several members of the “Friends of the Church” group for whom I am writing. The reactions are varied. Some are immediately quick to express the importance of the fellowship. This is very important to people who are involved outside the four walls of the church. The people with whom they come together are often friends from other places in life. The church allows some time for interaction when the subject of conversation does not have to be how high is the price of gasoline or what the new TV schedule includes. Often, it is the only social gathering there is in a week.

This has been more true in recent weeks since the closure of services at the church. When asked if they had a substitute church to worship, most quickly declared there wasn’t. So it’s back to virtual meetings. So be it.

The second most common reason given was spiritual growth. It’s a reminder of the importance of prayer—a commitment to worship that continues through the week. It’s the opportunity to listen to others who have been doing the same thing, and learning

Alternare Drawings are digital and paper drawings by different Artists from around the world

The drawings in Alternare are usually on a 3 1/2 inch by 3 1/2 inch square medium - representing a full size drawing in the artists universe

Each drawing is a new thought, a new gesture, a new concept in the vast universe of visual expression

No two Alternare Drawings will ever be the same as there is a vast multitude of different thought processes, different gesture experiences and a multitude of conceptual universes

The concept for Alternare originated as a series of square pieces of cardboard painted and drawn on by a small group of friends in the summer of 2008. After the first series of cardboard squares, Alternare wanted to expand and let more people join in and be a part of this experience. Alternare realized that the cardboard was heavy and cumbersome so digital forms were next.

Alternare is open to any artists from anywhere who would love to be a part of this unique and magical concept. It is hoped that Alternare will continue to expand and share its love of drawing and artistic expression with others.

The Art Of Logic: Axiotetems

Logic is not a mere game, it is a crucial tool for reasoning. If you're going to use it, you might as well use it well. The German philosopher Friedrich Schehl developed an interesting tool for organizing syllogistic arguments in 1877. It's called “Axiotetems”, and it provides an aesthetic and systematic guide to valid argument forms.

Syllogistic arguments are the building blocks of a reasoned discourse. As the Ancient Greeks recognized, logic is a fundamental tool for the formation and evaluation of arguments and as such, an indispensable component of philosophical, scientific, and legal inquiry.

The Axiotetem for the Fourteen Categorematic Codes illustrates a valid argument as a triangle, but only if the three terms form a Darii type syllogism, where both premises are “All X are Y” or “No X are Y”. Otherwise, there are no other valid Axiotem configurations, for example the argument formed by the terms “All birds can fly

It's hard being a man in today's world. Or a black man, at least. Take Denzel Washington's character in "The Man from Hollywood," the request of which to see at the library meeting was met with groans and negative head-shaking.

Denzel's character, Tom Wright, is a black screenwriter who goes to Hollywood looking for work and finds trouble. He also finds a girlfriend. She's played by Sandra Locke, a redhead who doesn't look at all like Denzel -- or, for that matter, like Sandra Locke.

In Hollywood, Tom's skin color works against him, as you can imagine. When he gets an audition, the producer asks him to change the part from black to white. In Canada, it turns out, it works against him, too, though for different reasons. In Canada, he's seen as a Hollywood man come to steal the ideas of struggling, struggling Canadian screenwriters.

A Pedagogy of Multilingualism: Towards a Transformative Language Ecology



In recent years, the concept of language ecology has been widely used in discussions on multilingualism. Language ecology refers to the set of relationships between language use and the socio-cultural and political context where the languages are used. In contrast to the traditional view of monolingualism, where the prevailing ideology has been that of assimilation, the current situation is such that communities are multi-ethnic and the ways in which languages are used include not only the use of different languages among different communities but also the use of languages by different community members within the same context.

Language policies in many countries are focused on maintaining and promoting monolingualism within a dominant framework. section describes features for database backend handlers, some using pypgm module or pyarrow module. PostGIS is a database extension for PostgreSQL to store and operate geographic objects.

If you’re creating a new database backend, consult the requests module for standard features. Geospatial database drivers (like PostGIS) support more geographical and spatial requests, use the geospatial decorators defined in the class geom.


This example shows how to create a query to get a point within a defined perimeter. The perimeter is defined within a geometry called ‘polygen’. Also shows how to generate a query parameter from other models fields.

    polygen = PostGISGeometryField(db_index=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.type

    name = models.CharField(max_length=50)
    address = models.ForeignKey(Address, on_delete=models.

Blog Post — 1 month/22-OCT-'18 0301 EDTDerik's Story

As the 2015-2020 Dietary Guidelines Advisory Committee began meeting, there was one piece of data in particular that really resonated with me. The data compared the consumption of different nutrients across generations. Specifically, it looked at the change in the average amount of added sugars consumed between the 2002-2004 National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) and the 2016-2018 NHANES. The data showed that over the 14-year period, the average consumption of added sugars by adults in this country increased by about 6 teaspoons daily, which translates to 29 pounds of added sugar over that same 14-year time period. As the youngest member of the Committee, I couldn’t help but think about the implications of that trend on my young children, who were just babies at the time of the 2002-2004 survey. Lenny Bruce (October 13, 1925 – August 3, 1966) was an American stand-up comedian, satirist, and social critic. In a career lasting less than a decade, he revolutionized 20th-century stand-up comedy, exerting increasing influence on subsequent generations of comics, musicians, writers, and performers. The First Amendment to the United States Constitution and free speech have become the stuff of his legend, and he was a catalyst in the 1960s counterculture, though his work remained controversial and was frequently banned from the stage.

	\section{Introduction}

In the past few years, pre-training language models (PLMs) on a large corpus have significantly improved various natural language tasks. BERT~\citep{devlin2018bert} and its variants~\citep{yang2019xlnet,clark2020electra,raffel2020exploring,brown2020language} pre-train representations with large amount of text, and then fine-tune the representations to specific downstream tasks. Most of these PLMs are based on the masked language modeling (MLM) objective where tokens/words in a text sequence are randomly dropped and the model predicts the dropped ones. This objective enables PLMs to understand the contextual meaning of a word, and has achieved great improvement on many tasks, such as natural language inference (NLI)~\cite{bowman-etal-2015-large}, text classification~\cite{zhang2015character}, and question answering~\cite{rajpurkar-etal-2016-squad,wang2018glue}.

Wondjonga by Richard Flanagan

I came across Richard Flanagan's novel Wondjastra (or Wondjonga) recently and found myself unable to put it down for many a night. Set against the back drop of the 19th century Australia, the novel weaves a complex tale of relationships, loyalties, and survival in the harsh outback.

The novel is set in the area around the Burrin Bears Station in South Australia. It tells the story of a young aboriginal boy named Wondjastra (also called Billy) and a young Irish man named Tom who end up forging an unlikely alliance in the face of the hostility and hatred of the white settlers. As the novel progresses, we move back and forth in time, exploring how this relationship came to be, and how it shaped both of these men's lives.

Goalkeeping Fitness And Health

Goalkeeping fitness and health is now a vital part of a modern goalkeeper’s preparation. It is an area of the sports world where the saying, ‘practice makes perfect’ does not apply. Skill and technique have to be absolutely perfect, but if they are not backed up by the physical fitness and health required to perform at the peak for 90 minutes, it won’t be enough.

Goalkeeping fitness and health

Goalkeeping is unlike any other position in football because of the nature of the movements and the demands placed on stamina, speed, agility, reaction time, and flexibility due to the position. The demands on the goalkeeper are different to the outfield athletes because of the nature of the role. The goalkeeper is the only outfield position where a peak performance can be played in the space of a few minutes.

Solar Cells as Renewable Energy for Model Railroads

Solar power for model railroads is a practical and renewable source of electricity. In this article, we’ll discuss how solar cells, also referred to as photovoltaic cells, work and provide tips on selecting and effectively using them on your model railroad.

How Solar Cells Work

A solar cell consists of a semiconductor layer sandwiched between two plates of metal. This semiconductor is typically a compound of purified silicon. When light from the sun, or any other source, is directed onto the semiconductor, the photons from the light excite the electrons in the silicon, causing them to break free from their atoms and flow as an electric current.

Solar cells generate direct current (DC) electricity, the same type of power used by model railroad electronics. The current is proportional to the amount of light energy absorbed by the semiconductor. On a dark or cloudy day, the amount of energy generated is significantly reduced.

Effective Use of Solar Cells for Model Railroads

There are a few important things to consider when deciding to use solar cells on your model railroad:

  1. Power Storage: Solar

Literate vs. Non-Literate
People with different levels of education think and see the-same-but-different:

Literate people with paper (or any other hard-copy material) in front of them:
- they feel confident
- they have answers
- they are the experts; in control

Non-literate people in similar context:
- they are not comfortable (or even scared)
- they lack confidence
- they feel like being outside the box
The pain with the difference is that literate people do not understand non-literate people's pain - and, thus, literate people often treat non-literate people like those "outsiders" they are.

Literate vs. Transliterate


The difference between literate and transliterate is not so much in what they are doing but how do they feel about what they are doing.

Lionfish Invasion - A Personal View

I have been asked recently about the lionfish invasion in the Caribbean. I can tell you from personal experience that it is a horrible situation. Dive sites that were once populated with schools of large fish are now dominated by lionfish. You don't have to be a marine biologist to see that they are having an impact. I have stood on the reef and watched juvenile damselfish, parrotfish, and other prey species scatter in fear from their presence. If you have never seen a lionfish, let me paint you a picture. They are large, venomous fish with a fan-like protrusion from their midsection covered with venomous tentacles. They are striped like a tiger for camouflage. They look like they would love to eat you if you weren't made of the wrong kind of flesh.

It is unfortunate that the media focuses on the invasive species without mentioning that our own practices are allowing it to happen. For the past 20 years

Posted: Saturday, April 4, 2009 5:08 PM

There is likely a script that runs on your system at regular intervals and renames the log file each time it runs. You can check if there is a cron job or task scheduler entry for Raccoon.log by reviewing the system maintenance plans.

Usually the script might be something like this:

1. Move the existing racoon.log file to racoon.log.1
2. Delete any existing racoon.log.n files where n is 2 or greater
3. Create a new racoon.log fileHome » Topics » hiv and aids

So I think every other Friday-ish, we like to talk about a weird little corner of our collective hive brain, which is to say, we're going to point you to some interesting news, social media posts, YouTube videos, or other things that some of our users have found inspiring. This week that includes a story about what it means to be social and to suffer for art.

First up, an interesting article from The New York Times titled "This Is the 'Social' in Social Science" by Benedict Carey. The piece points out that much of the scientific research that we use to back up our beliefs about human nature and society is based on social interactions, which seem to be more complicated and nuanced than previously assumed.

For instance, we often read research that claims the average person knows a couple of thousand to a couple of thousand-million people who might matter to them, but can probably list, with some gaps but not much, about 200 people in roughly the right order. IDR #48 - I Don ’ Tory’s Gum Line Is Beautiful - YouTube

 photo still extracted from the YouTube video showing the full-length of the artist's gum line.

I must admit, I was genuinely shocked when I learned that IDR #48 is about the beauty of a man’s gum line. However, I can honestly say that it got me to be more appreciative of oral hygiene and dental care. The YouTube video has an interesting concept with the artist using different techniques to make the viewer pay attention to the model's gum line.

The start of the video shows the model in a relaxed yet sensual pose. The lighting gives off a warm and welcoming feeling, showing that even the most personal of areas can be celebrated. The artist had chosen an orange color to accentuate the model's gum line, making it look more defined and attractive. • Axioms: Something you accept that we can know to be true without needing proof.
• Facts: Something that can be known, and is true. Facts are things discovered through empirical testing or by the use of reason.
• Logic: The study of valid reasoning.
• Reason: The critical and rational discernment and investigation of a subject matter by the application of logical reasoning.
• Truth: When something is true, what has been said or done is in accordance with the facts or reality. In other words, it is the truth when we know it is true.
• Opinion: When you have an opinion, you are expressing or believing what is true for you, even if not necessarily known as true.

Dear jtay46,

Welcome to the forum.  

It is possible for a cough to cause a snuffing sound. Any obstruction of the airways (pharynx, larynx, trachea) can cause this. There are several reasons for such an obstruction including:

1) Weak throat muscles - In some individuals, it is simply a weakness of certain throat muscles that causes the snuffing sound. This weakens muscles do not close your airways properly when you cough, resulting in a snuffing sound.

2) Narrow airways - Narrowing of the air passages (as in laryngotracheitis, bronchitis or laryngeal tumor) can result in snuffing cough.

3) Thickened secretions - Thick mucus produces a snuffing sound because it can obstruct air passages and force you to cough more forcefully in an effort to clear it.
